instruction: spades_compile.sh -> decouple from cmake step (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. For generic questions use Q&A section in the Discussions forum above. I just git cloned the current source of spades. If I understood the compilation process correctly, we have to first run spades_compile.sh. This will then compile spades. I would like to pass the prefix such as via: cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr . To install spades into the /usr/ prefix. We can do so already even with this .sh file; I run it first, the cmake files are created, then I can compile it into the /usr/ prefix. But this is not usually how software is installed. Normally we can run cmake in the toplevel directory, and pass the prefix into it. I had a look at that .sh file. It has about 247 lines. I did not see a simple way to pass the prefix to it. I would thus like to make, say, two suggestions. The first would be to simply put the cmake files into the project as-is, so that we can simply run it with the /usr/ prefix. I believe this should work fine on Linux. I compiled spades on my first attempt; boost I had already installed, the compilation worked. So the only problem I had was the prefix. Lateron I changed it and it worked too, but I also want to store spades into a local collection of software projects I can easily install, and eventually install it easily into, say, /Programs/Spades/06.02.2026/ e. g. in a format where I checked out the git sources the last time. The second suggestion is to decouple the .sh into two variants. One .sh simply generates the cmake files but does not run them. The old spades_compile.sh can be retained, but also will call this .sh file before it runs. If such a separate .sh file exists, I can call it, it generates the cmake files, then I can use cmake to compile it. I believe either of these two suggestions would be an improvement over the current status quo. The current status quo is that if you run the .sh file (spades_compile.sh) it will use the current working directory as a prefix:
output: > It has about 247 lines. I did not see a simple way to pass the prefix to it. Well, https://github.com/ablab/spades/blob/main/spades_compile.sh#L225 says: > cmake -G "Unix Makefiles" -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="$PREFIX" $ADDITIONAL_FLAGS "$BASEDIR/src" And we're having in the early beginning: ``` if [ "x$PREFIX" = "x" ]; then PREFIX="$(pwd)" fi ``` So just running `PREFIX=/usr/local/whatever/dir/you/would/want spades_compile.sj` would to the trick. In fact this is documented in the manual: https://ablab.github.io/spades/installation.html#downloading-and-compiling-spades-source-code The reason this script does exist is for users that are scared about cmake and other things to have single push-button solution with some sensible predefined defaults. Although it is just a wrapper over normal cmake invocation. Everyone else (who is aware of cmake and how software is built) could just use cmake directly. There is no need for any script, etc.

instruction: Splitter cannot create output directory even if the parent path exists (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I am testing the Splitter preprint release to see how it works. I tested the command with: ``` splitter assembly.gfa tell-seq.yaml out ``` And I got this error: ``` 0:00:00.000 1M / 6M INFO General (main.cpp : 348) Starting SpLitteR, built from N/A, git revision N/A 0:00:00.008 1M / 6M INFO General (main.cpp : 351) Maximum # of threads to use (adjusted due to OMP capabilities): 49 terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::filesystem::__cxx11::filesystem_error' what(): filesystem error: cannot create directory: No such file or directory [] Aborted (core dumped) ``` The three TellSeq read files exist (R1, R2, I1) and the output directory too (up to the parent). I tried pre-creating the `out` directory and then running the tool as well, but I get the same error. I am not understanding what is the path that does not exist, since the ones I pass exist (data, yaml file and output directory). I cannot attach logs or params since it is a SplitteR run. I hope you can help me! ### spades.log - ### params.txt - ### SPAdes version - ### Operating System Ubuntu 22.04 ### Python Version _No response_ ### Method of SPAdes installation - ### No errors reported in spades.log - [x] Yes
output: It seems that the issue is caused by not specifying the `--tmp-dir` option. Could you please add `--tmp-dir tmp` to the command line and test again?

instruction: Question on frame translation (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Hello, A naive question. Are the final transcripts from the RNASpades pipeline in 5' to 3' direction? I am wondering whether I have to translate in all six frames or translating in just three will suffice. This minor detail will affect computing costs for me. Please advice. Thanks Abhijit
output: @sanyalab If you data is not strand-specific, orientation of the final transcripts is random. Orientation can be derived by mapping reads back to transcripts and checking poly-A tail positions. However, mapping reads, as well as the de novo assembly itself, is surely way more computationally expensive than translating RNA sequences. However, if you imply some computationally expensive downstream protein analysis, then yes, mapping reads back to the assembly might help. Best Andrey

instruction: mmap(2) failed. Reason: Cannot allocate memory. Error code: 12 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Spades cannot allocate memmory while the system had enough memory and the settings allowed it. ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23016584/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23016586/params.txt) ### SPAdes version v4.0.0 and v4.2.0 ### Operating System ubuntu desktop/server 24.04 LTS ### Python Version 3.12.6 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [x] Yes

No further reason was given, there is no way to recover from this and therefore the only solution for SPAdes is to terminate. > > While you might be thinking that there is "enough memory", in practice there is not. Also, usually these high-memory systems are very poorly configured for apps that might consume lots of RAM. > > See discussion in [#871](https://github.com/ablab/spades/issues/871) I've tired changing the vm.max_map_count to 2147483642, but I'm encountering the same issue. mmap(2) failed. Reason: Cannot allocate memory. Error code: 12 By the way the vm.max_map_count was bumped to 1048576 in ubuntu 24.04 **I've captured the system settings, could you take a look?** These are default settings in ubuntu server 24.04 LTS, except for vm.max_map_count(1048576) and open files(1024). instruction: Does the memory flag change assembly logic ? (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Hi, we love spades and use it in Galaxy. We were wondering if the memory flag changes assembly logic in a way that the same file produces different assemblies when run with different -m paramters? The docs does only mention buffers but maybe also kmers can be adjusted ? This would break reproducibility and would require that the -m parameter needs to be fixed for reproducible workflows.
output: > We were wondering if the memory flag changes assembly logic in a way that the same file produces different assemblies when run with different -m paramters? No. SPAdes tries hard not to change the results based on aux. cmdline options. We saw some instabilities wrt some value accesses in the past in the 3-rd party libraries we are using. Though I do not think these apply anymore. In fact, our old CI used to contain job that verified same results despite different `-m` / `-t` being used.

instruction: Failure to estimate insert size and align pairs on a very small dataset (70kb)? (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hi, thank you for developing the SPAdes software. I encountered an error while using the software , which is shown as follows === Error correction and assembling warnings: 0:00:04.152 1M / 184M WARN General (pair_info_count.cpp : 177) Unable to estimate insert size for paired library #0 0:00:04.159 1M / 184M WARN General (pair_info_count.cpp : 183) None of paired reads aligned properly. Please, check orientation of your read pairs. 0:00:04.164 1M / 184M WARN General (repeat_resolving.cpp : 82) Insert size was not estimated for any of the paired libraries, repeat resolution module will not run. 0:00:05.243 1M / 184M WARN General (pair_info_count.cpp : 93) Single reads are not used in metagenomic mode ======= Warnings saved to /work/home/ac7yytb12r/result/virus/PRJNA762360/spades/SRR17421892/warnings.log After checking with fastp, I confirmed that the input file is a paired-end file, but its size is only 70kb. Could the error be caused by too few input sequences (poor data quality)? The following is the log generated during operation. Thank you for your help. ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21732793/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21732803/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes version: 4.1.0 ### Operating System Linux-3.10.0-957.el7.x86_64-x86_64-with-glibc2.17 ### Python Version Python version: 3.12.8 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [x] Yes
output: > Could the error be caused by too few input sequences (poor data quality)? Well, sure. The log clearly reads: > None of paired reads aligned properly. Please, check orientation of your read pairs. It seems your input consists only of 129 paired-end reads. It is highly unlikely that this is well-covered part of any genome. Even more, as far as I can see, your reads does not produce any meaningful assembly at all, the output is simple empty as it contained only 24 disconnected pieces not longer than your reads and therefore is not an assembly result. Hope this helps.

instruction: Duplicated headers when combining contigs from different samples (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. For generic questions use Q&A section in the Discussions forum above. I have noticed when I perform spades assembly per sample, I have an 1-10% chance that the contig header has an exact match with another header from a different sample. So what I now do, is re-opening and parsing all of those headers to rename them. This is required when I perform [contig annotation](https://github.com/MGXlab/CAT_pack/tree/master). ### Describe the solution you'd like I would like that the headers are replaced by a hash or other random generator that has less chance of creating a duplicate. At the moment a header that starts with `NODE_1_` is a bit too generic, and the numbering could be replaced by a random code. At the moment, my data is very fragmented, meaning this will result in millions of contigs. ### Describe alternatives you've considered The alternative for now, I have a python script that parses all samples, creates a mapping file of all contig headers and performs re-naming and re-writing of the contig files with the new header and the mapping file (to link the old name with the new name for each sample) ### Additional context _No response_
output: > I have noticed when I perform spades assembly per sample, I have an 1-10% chance that the contig header has an exact match with another header from a different sample. This is expected. The contig names are per-sample. Different downstream users have different requirements and the producer is not expected to be universal :) So, your current approach with post-assembly renaming is correct.

input: ### Description of bug Dear Spades team I just installed spades v4.1.0 in a conda environment at a RedHat 9.5 with CPU: 40x Intel(R) Xeon(R) Platinum 8176 CPU @ 2.10GHz Memory: 503.38 GiB. I am trying to assembly paired-end illumina reads. I run the tests using: spades.py --test And I get this: ``` [('/fast/AG_Forslund/Victor/data/EMBARK/spades_test/configs/hammer/config.info', '/fast/AG_Forslund/Victor/data/EMBARK/spades_test/corrected/configs/config.info', "[Errno 524] Unknown error 524: '/fast/AG_Forslund/Victor/data/EMBARK/spades_test/corrected/configs/config.info'"), ('/fast/AG_Forslund/Victor/data/EMBARK/spades_test/configs/hammer', '/fast/AG_Forslund/Victor/data/EMBARK/spades_test/corrected/configs', "[Errno 524] Unknown error 524: '/fast/AG_Forslund/Victor/data/EMBARK/spades_test/corrected/configs'")] Traceback (most recent call last): File "/home/vjarqui/miniforge3/envs/spades/bin/spades.py", line 624, in main pipeline.generate_configs(cfg, spades_home, tmp_configs_dir) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home/vjarqui/miniforge3/envs/spades/share/spades/spades_pipeline/stages/pipeline.py", line 44, in generate_configs stage.generate_config(cfg) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^ File "/home/vjarqui/miniforge3/envs/spades/share/spades/spades_pipeline/stages/error_correction_stage.py", line 169, in generate_config stage.generate_config(self.cfg) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home/vjarqui/miniforge3/envs/spades/share/spades/spades_pipeline/stages/error_correction_stage.py", line 67, in generate_config copy_tree(os.path.join(self.tmp_configs_dir, "hammer"), dst_configs, preserve_times=False) ~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home/vjarqui/miniforge3/envs/spades/share/spades/spades_pipeline/support.py", line 954, in copy_tree shutil.copytree(src, dst, copy_function = copy_fn) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home/vjarqui/miniforge3/envs/spades

This is why I suspected I/O issue. Maybe your system administrator could provide some insights / restrictions. Alternatively, you can try using `/fast` as a scratch directory passing it as `--tmpdir`.

instruction: rnaSPAdes with strand-specific data (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Hi! I would like to use SPAdes `--rna` mode to assemble the transcriptome of some bacterial RNA seq samples. After trimming, I have paired-end reads R1 and R2 (reverse-stranded). To do this, I used the following command: `spades.py --rna -t 32 -1 ./paired_R1.fastq.gz -2 ./paired_R2.fastq.gz --ss rf -o ./results` However, I also have some unpaired R1 and R2 reads resulting from trimming. [Documentation](https://ablab.github.io/spades/rna.html) says: > When assembling multiple strand-specific libraries, only the first one will be used to determine the strand of each transcript. Thus, we suggest not to mix data with different strand-specificity. If I used the unpaired reads from R1, there wouldn't be a problem, since the strandedness is maintained, but in R2 it would be the opposite. Therefore, it's not clear to me how I could use my four read files (paired_R1, paired_R2, unpaired_R1 and unpaired_R2) in a single command. **How could I do so?** If you can provide some example command(s) it would be of great help. I think a possible solution would be using `seqtk seq -r` with unpaired_R2 and merging unpaired_R1 and unpaired_R2, but I'd prefer using just one command. **In any case, is this correct?** I've also looked at the [Specifying multiple libraries with YAML data set file](https://ablab.github.io/spades/running.html#input-data) section, but it doesn't seem to contain a way to specify strandedness. Thanks in advance!
output: Sorry for the delay, but thank you so much for your help @andrewprzh! I've tried all four options (the one with only paired reads and the three you provided) and I was hoping that introducing unpaired reads would increase the number of contigs. This has been the case, but I was hoping the three options you provided would give identical results, but they haven't. Overall, the variation isn't very large, but I'd like to obtain a consensus transcriptome for all 18 samples. I think I'm more convinced by the following option (test 2 on the image), which is easier to understand: ` spades.py --rna -t 32 --pe1-1 ./paired_R1.fastq.gz --pe1-2 ./paired_R2.fastq.gz --pe1-s unpaired_R1.fastq.gz --pe1-s unpaired_R2.reverse_complemented.fastq.gz --ss rf -o ./results ` However, just for curiosity, I would like to know what causes these differences: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/085e8065-3fe4-44eb-805f-c940a4c8152e) Best regards, Eduardo.

instruction: `SyntaxWarning` for invalid escape sequences in Python 3.12 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug In Python >= 3.6, invalid escape sequences for Unicode strings emit a `DeprecationWarning`, [changed](https://docs.python.org/release/3.12.0/whatsnew/changelog.html) to a `SyntaxWarning` in Python 3.12, to finally become a `SyntaxError` in a future Python version. SPAdes uses several of these invalid escape sequences across the Python scripts, most notably (or only?) in regular expressions. I obtained two of these warnings for a metaSPAdes run, in: https://github.com/ablab/spades/blob/80f282ec864cd8f575c02d556c1f823c5b02e450/ext/src/python_libs/joblib3/func_inspect.py#L50-L52 and in: https://github.com/ablab/spades/blob/80f282ec864cd8f575c02d556c1f823c5b02e450/ext/src/python_libs/joblib3/_memory_helpers.py#L10 To my knowledge, `<` and `>` should have never carried a specific meaning in Python's regex flavor and thus shouldn't have been required to escape, whilst the character classes `\s` and `\d` indeed do. As the `SyntaxWarning` will eventually become a `SyntaxError`, SPAdes will break, in the future. Since the same error has already been reported in #1320 and in #1326, but was only selectively fixed in 60ad35e, it may be preferable to go through the code base and find all strings with escape sequences and fix them, either by doubling the backslashes for Python's parser, or, preferred, by marking them as raw strings. If you want, I could try fixing this myself *via* pull request. ### spades.log Since my dataset contains sensitive information (including the names of file paths), I cannot upload the `spades.log` and am only able to provide the following snippets. Since, however, the error should be clear, I hope more data isn't needed. ```yaml Command line: /opt/spades/bin/spades.py --meta --threads=32 --memory=200 -1 /path/to/r1.fastq.gz -2 /path/to/r2.fastq.gz -o /path/to/metaspades_out_sample_1 System information: SPAdes version: 4.1.0 Python version: 3.12.3 OS: Linux-5.4.0-208-generic-x86_64-with-glibc2.39 ``` ```python == Running: /usr/bi
output: Using the following two regexes ``` Regex 1: (?<!r)"[^"]*?\\[^0nrtux'"].*?" Regex 2: (?<!r)'[^']*?\\[^0nrtux'"].*?' ``` on all Python scripts, I found several more places with problematic escape sequences and tried to fix them now. The regexes also apply for doubled backslashes (which are valid), but they are rare enough not to flood the output. In most cases, the issues were indeed related to the `re` module, *i.e.*, to regexes, just in one case to `os.system`. Besides, I noticed several other issues maybe worth some attention, *viz.* Python 2.x code (which is not supported, anymore, and a security risk), like `print` statements or outdated `raise` and `except` expressions in *e.g.* [pydot.py](src/tools/webvis/pydot.py). Since the [manual](https://ablab.github.io/spades/installation.html) claims Python 3.8 as minimum requirement, the Python 2 code won't run, but I guess (without verification) that the respective libraries aren't in use for the core of SPAdes. `Pylance` reported several other errors, such as intermixed spaces and tabs in *e.g.* [texify_results.py](src/tools/contig_analysis/texify_results.py). Besides, there were instances with harmless but unnecessary trailing semicolons or unused module imports. The last thing I noticed were shebang lines given after the copyright header, like in [test.py](src/projects/mts/test.py). Since the kernel only interprets shebang lines when the `#!` makes up the first two bytes of an executable script, running `./test.py` would start an `sh` or `bash` instance (depending on the currently active shell), not `/usr/bin/python`. Of course, when the scripts are only used as libraries *via* `import`, the shebang line doesn't matter.

instruction: Keep contigs generated for the different kmers lenghts (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hi, I am using metaspades and do not understand how to keep all contigs generated for the different kmers lenghts given during assembly. Within the individual kmers folders (K21, K31... K101) I find the following files/directories: configs final.lib_data simplified_contigs Is there a way to keep them during assembly with metaspades? Thanks, best| ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19109611/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19109613/params.txt) ### SPAdes version Spades-3.15.3 ### Operating System version 5.0.17(1)-release (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu ### Python Version python-3.11.7 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [x] Yes
output: > Is there a way to keep them during assembly with metaspades? > Thanks, best| There are no "contigs generated for the different kmers lenghts". Per SPAdes manual (https://ablab.github.io/spades/output.html): > K<##>/ - directory containing intermediate files from the run with K=<##>. These files should not be used as assembly results; use resulting contigs/scaffolds in files mentioned above. The result of assembly process is always indicated in the log: ```log * Assembled contigs are in /save_projet/herbasoil/ASSEMBLY/L1-10_metaspades_out/contigs.fasta * Assembled scaffolds are in /save_projet/herbasoil/ASSEMBLY/L1-10_metaspades_out/scaffolds.fasta * ```

instruction: binspreader: Unknown scaffold --> binned: 0 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I sincerely appreciate all the developers. I followed the attached protocol (https://star-protocols.cell.com/protocols/2802) to use BinSPreader with the PacBio+Illumina assembly results generated using the following SPAdes command: spades.py -o spades_w_pac_hifi --meta -1 all_clean_1.fq -2 all_clean_2.fq --pacbio HiFi.filt.fastq -t 71 -m 900 --checkpoints all BinSPreader command: ~/SPAdes-4.1.0-Linux/bin/binspreader ../assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa comebin_bins/binning.tsv binspreader-Rcorr --dataset library.yaml -t 94 -Rcorr -r | tee binspreader-Rcorr.log I used gfa version 1.2 library.yaml: [ { orientation: "fr", type: "paired-end", right reads: ["/data/2025-03-01_test/illu_trim/all_clean_1.fq"], left reads: ["/data/2025-03-01_test/illu_trim/all_clean_2.fq"] } ] binning.tsv: [attached] However, all scaffolds were displayed as "Unknown scaffold: NODE_xx_length_xx_cov_xx," and all edges were assigned as unbinned. 0:13:48.587 4997M / 20G INFO General (binning.cpp : 168) Unknown scaffold: NODE_223736_length_1002_cov_2.431890 0:13:49.019 5183M / 20G INFO General (binning_refiner.cpp : 334) Initial binning: Total bins: 429 Total edges: 6093917, binned: 0, unbinned: 6093917 (100%) [binning.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19101166/binning.zip) ### spades.log [binspreader-Rcorr.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19101240/binspreader-Rcorr.zip) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19101262/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes 4.1.0 ### Operating System Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS ### Python Version Python 3.12.2 ### Method of SPAdes installation binaries ### No errors reported in spades.log - [x] Yes
output: > [@mnter](https://github.com/mnter) [#1454](https://github.com/ablab/spades/pull/1454) should fix the issue proper. > > Note that since your assembly is pretty fragmented absent jump links do matter a lot. Thank you for the fix. I am testing Binspreader with the update. However, adding -DSPADES_ENABLE_PROJECTS=binspreader to spades_compile.sh did not install Binspreader, so I modified the command to cmake -G "Unix Makefiles" -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="$PREFIX" $ADDITIONAL_FLAGS -DSPADES_ENABLE_PROJECTS="spades;binspreader;spaligner;spades_tools;pathracer" "$BASEDIR/src". I sincerely appreciate your help once again and wish you success in your work.

instruction: Still encountering issue with Spades-Hammer K-mer counting (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I have been trying to resolve an issue with the K-mer counting for over two months, with no success. For some datasets, this occurs on every sample, therefore each sample fails. For others, only some samples fail. The issue reads: ERROR K-mer Counting (kmer_data.cpp : 353) The reads contain too many k-mers to fit into available memory. You need approx. 42.76GB of free RAM to assemble your dataset This suggests I am running out of RAM. However, 42GB is an incredibly low amount, and each is being run on nodes with between 3 and 6 TB available. The code has been modified further to assign at least 1TB of the available RAW to each SPAdes run. We have also looked at output from an earlier run that failed, and at no point during the failed run did it go above 3GB of RAM out of 3000GB. We know that it was probably running spades-hammer when it failed. It isn't running out of RAM unless whatever function spades-hammer uses to allocate memory is failing to allocate memory for some reason and the "out of memory" error is a red herring. The system did not return params.txt or spades.log files for the most recent run, but I include old files as an example (the error is the same). Please see the MissingSpades.o2488391 file attached for the most recent run where 18 samples tried to run and all failed. [MissingSpades.o2488391.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18618181/MissingSpades.o2488391.txt) Really hope you can assist to solve this issue. I have tried both in and out of Phyluce, and the error persists. Bw/ Jay ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18618183/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18618184/params.txt) ### SPAdes version Spades 3.12.0 ### Operating System Windows 10 22H2 ### Python Version Python 2.7.5 ### Method of SPAdes installation Conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [x] Yes
output: The log clearly reads: ``` Memory limit (in Gb): 8 ``` And indeed, the command line is: ``` Command line: /usr/local/miniconda/envs/phyluce-1.7.1/bin/spades.py --careful --sc --memory 8 --threads 12 ... ``` Note that you're explicitly setting hard memory limit to 8 Gb, hence the error. Refer to SPAdes manual for more information: https://ablab.github.io/spades/running.html#advanced-options

instruction: Numerous errors during Spades running (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hello, There are two errors and one warning in the attached `Spades` log file. ``` 1. ```mimalloc: error: thread 0x7f294b7af640: unable to allocate OS memory (67108864 bytes, error code: 12 [Cannot allocate memory], address: (nil), large only: 0, allow large: 1) mimalloc: process [info:](url) elapsed: 160563.809 s process: user: 1224599.957 s, system: 14503.077 s, faults: 26306211 rss: current: 579801792512, peak: 480573063168 commit: current: 579801792512, peak: 579801792512 ``` The above error is clearly related to the lack of required `RAM`. Is there a way from the `log` file i already have to figure out an approximate `RAM` amount i need for the successful `Spades` run? Besides, i'm wondering what is the actual peak of memory 480573063168 or 579801792512? Can you recommend a tool to check `RAM` consumption during `Spades` run or the information which is already present in the `log` file is sufficient for that? 2. Why i can not to save the `check point` and got the `warning`: `* 0:00:00.028 1M / 18M WARN StageManager (stage.cpp : 162) No saved checkpoint` 3. `* 1:35:39.164 9053M / 9053M ERROR General (kmer_splitter.hpp : 151) I/O error! Incomplete write! Reason: No space left on device. Error code: 28` There is approximately `1.5 Tb` of free space on the mounted file storage i use on my `virtual machine`. What is the possible reason for that error message and how to fix it? Many thanks, Denis ### spades.log [spades_out-5.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18218780/spades_out-5.txt) ### params.txt [params-3.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18218782/params-3.txt) ### SPAdes version 4.0.0 ### Operating System Ubuntu 22.04.3 LTS ### Python Version 3.13.1 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Hello > Is there a way from the log file i already have to figure out an approximate RAM amount i need for the successful Spades run? The amount of RAM required depends on many factors including (but not limited to) genome size, repeat content & complexity, ploidy & strain contents, error rate, etc. It is not possible to estimate this in advance. And it is not possible to estimate the amount of RAM required in advance as well. > Besides, i'm wondering what is the actual peak of memory The peak resident set size was approx 440 before memory allocation failed. And your OS failed to fulfil SPAdes request to allocate 64M more. > Can you recommend a tool to check RAM consumption during Spades run There are multiple tools available around for measuring memory consumption including e.g. `top`, `htop`, `atop`, etc. You may always consult with your system administrator what is available on a particular system. SPAdes-internal monitoring provides SPAdes-internal way, but certainly does not account for e.g. OS-imposed overheads, etc. > Why i can not to save the check point and got the warning: Checkpoints are disabled by default. Refer to [manual](https://ablab.github.io/spades/running.html#pipeline-options) for description of checkpoint-related options. > I/O error! Incomplete write! Reason: No space left on device. Error code: 28 > There is approximately 1.5 Tb of free space on the mounted file storage i use on my virtual machine. What is the possible reason for that error message and how to fix it? Well, the error is clear: write failed and this was the error message provided by your system. If you're suspecting some system misconfiguration, you might want to ask your system administrator.

instruction: error in metaplasmidspades --assembly-graph (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Regarding the –assembly-graph parameter: I have encountered an issue when running metaplasmidspades.py with the --assembly-graph parameter. The error message displayed is: == Error == To run read error correction, reads should be in FASTQ format. I have tried various combinations but have not found the correct input format. Additionally, I couldn’t find relevant examples in the user manual. Could you please clarify how to properly use the --assembly-graph parameter? ### spades.log python ../SPAdes-4.0.0-Linux/bin/metaplasmidspades.py -1 ../cleandata/P_Cu_2/P_Cu_2_R1.fq.gz -2 ../cleandata/P_Cu_2/P_Cu_2_R2.fq.gz --assembly-graph P_Cu_2/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa -k 21,33,55 -o P_Cu_2_plasmid_test -t 24 -m 1000 == Error == to run read error correction, reads should be in FASTQ format (.fq, .fastq, .bam, .fq.gz, .fastq.gz are supported): /mnt/sdb/yemao/wuhaowei/spades/P_Cu_2/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa (single reads, library number: 1, library type: assembly-graph) python ../SPAdes-4.0.0-Linux/bin/metaplasmidspades.py --assembly-graph P_Cu_2/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa -k 21,33,55 -o P_Cu_2_plasmid_test -t 24 -m 1000 == Error == to run read error correction, reads should be in FASTQ format (.fq, .fastq, .bam, .fq.gz, .fastq.gz are supported): /mnt/sdb/yemao/wuhaowei/spades/P_Cu_2/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa (single reads, library number: 1, library type: assembly-graph) python ../SPAdes-4.0.0-Linux/bin/metaplasmidspades.py --assembly-graph P_Cu_2/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa -k 21,33,55 -o P_Cu_2_plasmid_test -t 24 -m 1000 == Error == to run read error correction, reads should be in FASTQ format (.fq, .fastq, .bam, .fq.gz, .fastq.gz are supported): /mnt/sdb/yemao/wuhaowei/spades/P_Cu_2/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa (single reads, library number: 1, library type: assembly-graph) python ../SPAdes-4.0.0-Linux/bin/metaplasmidspades.py -1 ../cleandata/P_Cu_2/P_Cu_2_R1.fq.gz -2 ../cleandata/P_Cu_2/P_Cu_2_R2.fq.gz --asse
output: So, the documentation says: >--assembly-graph <file_name> File with assembly graph. Could only be used in plasmid, metaplasmid, metaviral and biosynthetic mode. The primary purpose of this option is to run these pipelines on already constructed and simplified assembly graphs, thus skipping a large part of SPAdes pipeline. Original reads the graph was constructed from need to be specified as well. Exact k-mer length (via -k option) should be provided. Note that the output would be different as compared to standalone runs of these pipelines as they set up graph simplification options as well. So, you need: - Specify original reads - Specify original single k-mer length - Skip read error correction - Graph should be in GFA format In short: this is quite advanced option used in "manual` mode.

instruction: metaspades hangs on k53 when in snakemake workflow (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Using a spades installation from `conda` and incorporated into a Snakemake workflow, the assembler hangs indefinitely when doing the `k33` or `k55` iteration in the workflow **but runs to completion when running spades manually outside of snakemake**. For clarity below, the `k_param` is `'auto'`. This is different from previous reported bugs that say spades hangs during error correction, as my issue seems to be at the assembly stage. I specifically split the two steps as so: ```yaml rule error_correction: input: FQ_R1 = outdir + "/fastq_preproc/input.R1.fq.gz", FQ_R2 = outdir + "/fastq_preproc/input.R2.fq.gz" output: outdir + "/error_correction/corrected/input.R1.fq00.0_0.cor.fastq.gz", outdir + "/error_correction/corrected/input.R2.fq00.0_0.cor.fastq.gz", outdir + "/error_correction/corrected/input.R_unpaired00.0_0.cor.fastq.gz" params: outdir = outdir + "/error_correction", k = k_param, mem = max_mem // 1000, extra = extra log: outdir + "/logs/error_correct.log" conda: f"{envdir}/assembly.yaml" threads: workflow.cores resources: mem_mb=max_mem shell: "metaspades.py -t {threads} -m {params.mem} -k {params.k} {params.extra} -1 {input.FQ_R1} -2 {input.FQ_R2} -o {params.outdir} --only-error-correction 2> {log}" rule spades_assembly: default_target: True input: FQ_R1C = outdir + "/error_correction/corrected/input.R1.fq00.0_0.cor.fastq.gz", FQ_R2C = outdir + "/error_correction/corrected/input.R2.fq00.0_0.cor.fastq.gz", FQ_UNC = outdir + "/error_correction/corrected/input.R_unpaired00.0_0.cor.fastq.gz" output: f"{outdir}/metaspades/contigs.fasta" params: outdir = outdir + "/metaspades", k = k_param, mem = max_mem // 1000, extra = extra log: outdir + "/logs/metaspades_assembly.log" conda: f"{envdir}/assembly.yaml" threads: workflow.cores resources: mem_mb=max_mem shell: "metaspades.py -t {threads} -m {params.mem} -k {params.k} {params.extra} -1 {input.FQ_R1C} -2 {input.FQ_R2C} -s {input.FQ_UNC} -o {params.outdir} --only-assembler 2> {log}" ``` ### s
output: > @asl conversely, couldn't the conda recipe use the precompiled binaries in the build instead of building it during the conda deployment? We do not maintain conda recipes, this is for conda folks to decide. Historically there were some issues with binaries as conda tries to "abstract out" the platform a bit. While this seems to work in 99% of cases, there are some caveats as it seems :)

instruction: Cannot open temporary file for writing. OS return value: 108 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hello! I'm running spades (metaspades) on a cluster server to co-assemble 25 paired-end metagenomic samples. Based on the log it seemed to be running fine, and I've run the same job script on subsets of 5 of the same samples with no problem. However, during the K21 assembly it encountered the following error: 4:09:51.337 41G / 59G ERROR General (kmer_splitter.hpp : 147) Cannot open temporary file /lustre07/scratch/danross/metawrapJL/ALL/ASSEMBLY/tmp/spades_82ecu3os/construction_9bxNl9/kmers_raw_ky839K.24 for writing == Error == system call for: "['/cvmfs/soft.computecanada.ca/easybuild/software/2023/x86-64-v3/Compiler/gcccore/spades/3.15.4/bin/spades-core', '/lustre07/scratch/danross/metawrapJL/ALL/ASSEMBLY/K21/configs/config.info', '/lustre07/scratch/danross/metawrapJL/ALL/ASSEMBLY/K21/configs/mda_mode.info', '/lustre07/scratch/danross/metawrapJL/ALL/ASSEMBLY/K21/configs/meta_mode.info']" finished abnormally, OS return value: 108 None It was using less than 2% of the available RAM and in a folder it should have every permission to write in. No other information is available. My samples are quite large, based on individual sample assembly I will need multiple TB of RAM - I've been waiting a month for my job to be accepted only to run into this error. Please help me figure out what happened. My command line code is below. spades.py \ -1 ../CLEAN_READS/ALL_READS_1.fastq \ -2 ../CLEAN_READS/ALL_READS_2.fastq \ -m 4000 \ -t 64 \ --meta \ --only-assembler \ -o ../ASSEMBLY ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17215177/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17215181/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.15.4 ### Operating System Linux-4.18.0-553.16.1.el8_10.x86_64-x86_64-AMD_EPYC_7502_32-Core_Processor-with-glibc2.37 ### Python Version 3.11.4 ### Method of SPAdes installation Unsure, on a third party cluster ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: The log reads: ``` 4:09:51.337 41G / 59G ERROR General (kmer_splitter.hpp : 147) Cannot open temporary file /lustre07/scratch/danross/metawrapJL/ALL/ASSEMBLY/tmp/spades_82ecu3os/construction_9bxNl9/kmers_raw_ky839K.24 for writing ``` This is an I/O problem on your cluster, not the SPAdes one. Maybe there is a limit on how many files you can open / write at once – oftentimes these clusters are very poorly configured. I would suggest you to contact your system administrator

instruction: Error when using conda spades (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug When I try to run spades.py --test or any other spades script, I get the following error: ``` == Warning == No assembly mode was specified! If you intend to assemble high-coverage multi-cell/isolate data, use '--isolate' option. Command line: /home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/bin/spades.py --test System information: SPAdes version: 3.15.3 Python version: 3.12.4 OS: Linux-5.15.0-118-generic-x86_64-with-glibc2.35 Output dir: /home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/ccit_denovo/spades_test Mode: read error correction and assembling Debug mode is turned OFF Dataset parameters: Standard mode For multi-cell/isolate data we recommend to use '--isolate' option; for single-cell MDA data use '--sc'; for metagenomic data use '--meta'; for RNA-Seq use '--rna'. Reads: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/bin/spades.py", line 659, in <module> main(sys.argv) File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/bin/spades.py", line 599, in main print_params(log, log_filename, command_line, args, cfg) File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/bin/spades.py", line 327, in print_params print_used_values(cfg, log) File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/bin/spades.py", line 117, in print_used_values dataset_data = pyyaml.load(open(cfg["dataset"].yaml_filename)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/share/spades/pyyaml3/__init__.py", line 72, in load return loader.get_single_data() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/share/spades/pyyaml3/constructor.py", line 37, in get_single_data return self.construct_document(node) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/share/spades/pyyaml3/constructor.py", line 46, in construct_document for dummy in generator: File "/home2b/eduardo.gonzalez/miniconda3/envs/denovo/share/spades/pyyaml3/constructor.py", l
output: Hi Eduardo, I have used SPAdes before on a different system but after installing it from conda I had the very same problem as you did. For some reason conda also installed an older version on my system. I now have v3.15.5 and it works for me Try uninstalling SPAdes using "conda uninstall spades" Then reinstall a newer version using "conda install -c bioconda -c conda-forge spades=3.15.5" (Some dependencies of SPAdes are in the Conda-forge channnel) Of course you could also test the very new v4.0. Since you are using both short and long reads I would reommend giving Rnabloom a shot. Basically, it will perform a long read assembly and only use the short reads for error correction. However, it's results strongly depend on the coverage by the long reads. If the coverage is low it is probably better to stick to assemblers like SPAdes or Trinity, that rely more on the information coming from short reads.

output: > Well, per SPAdes manual (https://ablab.github.io/spades/input.html): > > > > > If you have merged some of the reads from your paired-end (not mate-pair or high-quality mate-pair) library (using tools s.a. [BBMerge](https://jgi.doe.gov/data-and-tools/bbtools/bb-tools-user-guide/bbmerge-guide/) or [STORM](https://bitbucket.org/yaoornl/align_test/overview)), you should provide the file with resulting reads as a "merged read file" for the corresponding library. I just want to be sure that spades is using merged file (A1.fq.gz) for assembly and if the message that merged file was ignored is for other reasons.

output: Well, the error is: ``` mmap(2) failed. Reason: Cannot allocate memory. Error code: 12" ``` So, SPAdes was unable to allocate memory. In fact, it is your OS that did not fulfil SPAdes' request for memory allocation. SPAdes neither can workaround this error nor it could proceed.

instruction: == Error == system call for: finished abnormally, OS return value: 21 (Pair of read files contain unequal amount of reads) (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Pair of read files "/home/ysein/nfs/Residential_training/results/mapping_no_vector/unaligned_reads/15-B104_230113_L004_R1.cleaned_unaligned.fastq.gz" and "/home/ysein/nfs/Residential_training/results/mapping_no_vector/unaligned_reads/15-B104_230113_L004_R2.cleaned_unaligned.fastq.gz" contain unequal amount of reads == Error == system call for: "['/home/ysein/.conda/envs/spades/bin/spades-hammer', '/home/ysein/nfs/Residential_training/results/spades_assembly_results/corrected/configs/config.info']" finished abnormally, OS return value: 21 ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16462122/spades.log) ### params.txt [spades_assembly_and_variant_calling.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16462188/spades_assembly_and_variant_calling.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes v4.0.0 ### Operating System HPC: Linux-3.10.0-1160.62.1.el7.x86_64-x86_64-with-glibc2.17 ### Python Version Python 3.12.4 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: > The trimming and preprocessing procedures were paired-end aware. Here are the fastqc files showing that the R1 and R2 correspond. We have trimmed using fast, trimmomatic, and trim galore and for each the SPAdes assembly it would be terminated at this spot. Well, then you need to find out which step of your pipeline produces unequal read count. Note that your report mentioned "15-B104_230113_L004_R1.cleaned.fastq.gz" though the error message is about "15-B104_230113_L004_R1.cleaned_unaligned.fastq.gz", these are different files. Taking the educated guess about typical user issues: you tried to align reads to some reference, then took only unaligned ones and tried to assemble them? Then you need to ensure you are doing proper steps when only one mate is aligned and another one is not, i.e. you need to ensure you're picking either both reads from the pair or none, but not a single one.

instruction: Input requirement for hybrid assembly (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hi, I was running hybrid assembly with Illumina short reads + PacBio ccs. My short reads are merged into a single fastq after QC pipeline, consisting of both paired and orphan reads, so I specified -s because that was the only way it could run. It was running fine until it had the below error when SPAdes started to first run k21 assembling. The command I was running was: `/n/home13/marinachen/.conda/envs/spades/bin/spades.py --meta --pacbio /n/holystore01/LABS/huttenhower_lab/Users/mchen/data/PB_MGX/use/Soil_pool.hifi_reads.fastq.gz -s /n/holystore01/LABS/huttenhower_lab/Users/mchen/data/Illumina_MGX_forPB/Clean_data/Soil_Pool_S52_L001.fastq -o /n/holystore01/LABS/huttenhower_lab/Users/mchen/outputs/PB_MGX/hybrid_assembly/Soil_hybrid` And the error message was: > /0:00:00.007 1M / 32M ERROR General (pipeline.cpp : 216) Sorry, current version of metaSPAdes can work either with single library (paired-end only) or in hybrid paired-end + (TSLR or PacBio or Nanopore) mode. It looked like there was an issue with recognizing the input files for hybrid paired-end + PacBio mode? Thank you for any help! ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16318479/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16318481/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 4.0.0 ### Operating System Cannon HPC (linux) ### Python Version 3.10.9 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [ ] Yes
output: > My short reads are merged into a single fastq after QC pipeline, consisting of both paired and orphan reads. Don't do that. You need to provide proper paired-end dataset: either interleaved or in two separate files. > Sorry, current version of metaSPAdes can work either with single library (paired-end only) or in hybrid paired-end + (TSLR or PacBio or Nanopore) mode. This is expected. You need to have a paired-end library, not a single-end one (`-s`). See https://ablab.github.io/spades/input.html#paired-read-libraries for more information

instruction: Resuming Hybrid Assembly fails (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug When resuming a hybrid assembly job with `spades.py -o outdir/ --continue` for a hybrid job, the job fails with ``` == Error == something went wrong and file with corrected reads (/home/biomineruser/coassembly/SU03052702/spades_out/corrected/SU03052702_S19_R_unpaired00.0_0.cor.fastq) is missing! ``` But the corrected directory looks like ``` 4096 Jul 17 06:16 ../ 39454348546 Jul 16 20:17 SU03052702_S19_R1_001_paired_human_clean.fastq00.0_0.cor.fastq 39236860650 Jul 16 20:17 SU03052702_S19_R2_001_paired_human_clean.fastq00.0_0.cor.fastq 50487778 Jul 16 20:17 SU03052702_S19_R_unpaired00.0_0.cor.fastq.gz 4096 Jul 17 06:16 configs/ 504 Jul 16 20:17 corrected.yaml 142 Jul 17 06:16 dont_correct.yaml 294 Jul 17 06:16 to_correct.yaml ``` This looks like the script is expecting all files to be uncompressed, but the unpaired reads is already compressed. ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16261124/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16261126/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes version: 4.0.0 ### Operating System OS: Linux-6.5.0-1024-azure-x86_64-with-glibc2.35 ### Python Version Python version: 3.12.2 ### Method of SPAdes installation Downloaded binaries with wget ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: > This looks like the script is expecting all files to be uncompressed, but the unpaired reads is already compressed. Yes. Unfortunately, the compression procedure is assumed to be atomic, however, if it is terminated, then everything might left in middle state. I would suggest you to uncompress the unpaired reads and restart. Also, installing `pigz` will make compression faster

instruction: Memory Limitation/ Finished abnormally (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I have been trying to run metaSPAdes on an HPC. Despite allocating **1.6 TB** of memory and using the **-m 1600** flag and **-t 40**, the SPAdes process consistently and automatically stops when memory usage approaches 250 GB. Continuing the script results in the same issue. I would appreciate any suggestions or solutions to resolve this problem. ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16045529/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16045531/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes 4.0.0 ### Operating System macOS 14.5 (23F79) ### Python Version Python 3.9.18 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [ ] Yes
output: So the log reads: ``` mimalloc: error: unable to allocate OS memory (67108864 bytes, error code: 12 [Cannot allocate memory], address: (nil), large only: 0, allow large: 1) mimalloc: process info: elapsed: 77602.980 s process: user: 76609.571 s, system: 708.610 s, faults: 388381 rss: current: 300456214528, peak: 268354465792 commit: current: 300456214528, peak: 300456214528 ``` This means that your OS failed to fulfil SPAdes request for memory allocation. This is outside any SPAdes control and there is no workaround. Practice shows that these large-memory HPC systems are very often misconfigured: they do not allow you to allocate the memory they have, effectively only some part is available for a single application. You can check https://github.com/ablab/spades/issues/871 for some discussion of system parameters that you (or your system administrator) might want to tune.

instruction: After upgrading to python3.12 : Error importing LooseVersion from distutils. Now needs to come directly from looseversion. (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input:
### Discussed in https://github.com/ablab/spades/discussions/1265
Originally posted by **haslamdb** March 25, 2024 Hi, I upgraded to python3.12 and was getting the error below when trying to run spades.py. It seems to be because distutils is no longer supported in python3.12 (setuptools is used instead). The workaround was to install looseversion with pip and change the import line in SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux/share/spades/spades_pipeline/support.py" changed: from distutils.version import LooseVersion to: from looseversion import LooseVersion. Original error message:Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/local/SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux/bin/spades.py", line 26, in import support File "/usr/local/SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux/share/spades/spades_pipeline/support.py", line 23, in from distutils.version import LooseVersion ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'distutils'
output: Hello You can use `next` branch that does not contain `distutils` anymore. This will be a base for the next SPAdes release

I don't know what happened but it did the trick for me. Hope this is useful to somebody else too.

instruction: Guidance: use of rnaspades in a study with matching microbial metagenomes and metatranscriptomes (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ## Description Hi, I'm a long-term fan of the SPAdes suite. In a study where matching metagenomes and metatranscriptomes are sequenced from microbial communities, and high quality MAGs are already obtained, I was wondering whether using `rnaspades` is a recommended way to assembly transcripts? By running `rnaspades` in a trial, I'm getting transcripts with length over 30kb, which makes me pause and consider whether I'm taking the right approach. Is it common to have such long transcripts? I'm also considering using predicted coding sequences (as proxy for transcripts) from either contigs or MAGs with `--careful ` and `--trusted-contigs ` or `--untrusted-contigs ` for further improvement of the assembled transcripts in `rnaspades`. ## Describe the solution you'd like Could you share your thoughts on whether this is a valid approach? ## Additional context ### Length the initially assembled transcripts: ``` cat transcripts.fasta | grep ">" | head -100 >NODE_1_length_56125_cov_42.650095_g0_i0 >NODE_2_length_47681_cov_169.986226_g1_i0 >NODE_3_length_43177_cov_160.376167_g0_i1 >NODE_4_length_42213_cov_340.366844_g2_i0 >NODE_5_length_42191_cov_324.270730_g2_i1 >NODE_6_length_41397_cov_335.196414_g2_i2 >NODE_7_length_40672_cov_217.244395_g0_i2 >NODE_8_length_40379_cov_346.417420_g3_i0 >NODE_9_length_40331_cov_376.643966_g3_i1 >NODE_10_length_40222_cov_225.048510_g0_i3 ... ``` ### Code for the initial assembly ``` function run_rnaspades(){ export PATH="/home/gnii0001/rp24/gaofeng/tools/SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux/bin:$PATH" echo "started assembly with rna on `date`" rnaspades.py -1 "$read_dir"/"$forward" -2 "$read_dir"/"$reverse" -t 96 -m 1600 -k 27,37,47,57,67,77,87,97,107,117,127 -o "$out_dir"/coassembly echo "completed assembly with rna on `date`" } ``` ### Code to reassemble with coding sequences from MAGs or contigs ``` function run_rnaspades_reassemble(){ export PATH="/home/gnii0001/rp24/gaofeng/tools/SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux/bin:$PATH" echo "started assembly with rna on `date`" rn
output: Dear @ganiatgithub If you have high-quality MAG, some reference-based / annotation based approach should give better results, probably even noticeably better. Something like, e.g. StringTie, might work. However, reference-based transcriptome assemblers are typically developed for eukaryotes and look for spliced isoforms. As to long genes - could it be contamination or operons containing multiple transcripts? We also have developed MetaGT pipeline, which assembles transcripts using rnaSPAdes and maps them to the metagenome assembly and matches against the prokka annotation. You may give it try as well. Best Andrey

instruction: How to calculate the coverage in the contig? (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I use the metaspades to assemble the pair-end reads and get the contig.fata. You could use your favourite scripting language to extract the information from FASTA headers. > > For example: > > ```shell > grep NODE contigs.fasta | awk 'BEGIN { FS = "_" }; { print substr($1, 2)"_"$2"\t"$6 }' > ``` > > or > > ```shell > grep NODE contigs.fasta | awk 'BEGIN { FS = "_" }; { print substr($0, 2)"\t"$6 }' > ``` > > Would yield tab-separate list of contig names and corresponding coverages (depending on what you consider as contig name) Sorry, I may not have described my problem clearly. I just want to known the "NODE_933_length_1534_cov_4.395538" term, what the mean "cov_4.395538"? and How to get this value?

instruction: Performance statistics, where? (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. For generic questions use Q&A section in the Discussions forum above. hello, it is said that as of version 3.14 there is performance statistics available. where, exactly? I am interested in the final runtime and amount of ram memory used for the process of metaspades assembly. thank you ### Describe the solution you'd like i guess a simple line in the log file would be enough. ### Describe alternatives you've considered _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_
output: You can certainly measure whatever performance statistics are relevant for your case and for your platform (as e.g. memory consumption could be really OS-dependent) as well as runtime. Sill, lots of statistics were always readily available from the log, e.g.: ``` 0:00:02.286 8M / 317M INFO General (pipeline.cpp : 287) SPAdes finished 0:00:02.287 1M / 127M INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 0 minutes 2 seconds ``` Here one can see the runtime of one SPAdes iteration as well as current and maximum RAM consumption. Overall running time could be obtained via system-standard `time` command.

instruction: How to correctly input merged files from two references for assembly? (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hello, I am interested in assembling a specific region of a genome for a single sample. I used the original filtered Illumina R1 and R2 fastq files to map to two different references for this specific region. I merged the 2 bam files, removed duplicates with picard, converted back to fastq, then tried to input for SPAdes but have the error below. How should I input this data so it will output an assembly of only the region I am interested in (not the entire genome)? Any advice appreciated! I am wondering if I need to use the read names in this merged fastq to create two new files as the R1 and R2 input fastq files. Is there a better route I am missing? ### spades.log (base) CombineMat1-12$ spades.py -o BU9_Mat_assembly2 --careful -t 32 -m 800 --12 BU9_CombinedMat.fastq == Warning == No assembly mode was specified! If you intend to assemble high-coverage multi-cell/isolate data, use '--isolate' option. instruction: SPAdes hangs intermittently on Rocky8/kernel 4.18 when MI_OVERRIDE=ON (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Recently we upgraded the OS on our cluster from CentOS 7.9 (kernel 3.10) to Rocky 8.8 (kernel 4.18). After this, we saw that the `spades-core` process would sometimes go into an uninterruptible sleep. For a certain test case, this would happen 8 out of 10 times on the new OS, while on the old OS it always runs correctly. The last line in the output of the failed runs differs from run to run, so it does not seem to be the case that it is hanging at a specific line of code. It does not seem to be a lack of memory, since the successful runs consume about 25GB out of the available 180GB. On the node where this happens, following message appears: ```INFO: task spades-core:17535 blocked for more than 120 seconds.``` When running `spades-core` with `strace -f`, these are the last lines of a hanging run: ``` [pid 1682422] getcpu([11], [0], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682411] getcpu([22], [1], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682406] getcpu([35], [1], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682418] getcpu([1], [0], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682393] getcpu([5], [0], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682392] getcpu([10], [0], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682423] getcpu([32], [1], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682417] getcpu([23], [1], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682404] getcpu([9], [0], NULL) = 0 [pid 1682404] mmap(0x4609b000000, 268435456, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_NORESERVE, -1, 0 ``` Since this pointed to a problem with memory mapping, I recompiled SPAdes configuring it with `MI_OVERRIDE=OFF` (see https://github.com/microsoft/mimalloc/issues/535#issuecomment-1020553023). With this new compilation, the same test case runs fine (10 out of 10 times) on the new OS. I am not entirely sure what is happening: * A process that goes into uninterruptible sleep can indicate I/O problems. I have tried several file systems for writing output and the outcome is always the same. A filesystem problem would also not explain why `MI_OVERRIDE=OFF` solves the problem. * The problem cannot purely come from SPAdes, since it does run correctly on the old OS and when
output: Thanks for your feedback. We can keep this issue closed, but I still wanted to mention I see lower memory consumption and better performance when using the executable compiled with `MI_OVERRIDE=OFF`: ``` MI_OVERRIDE=ON (default) - timings for K21,K33,K55,K77 0:03:33.350 1M / 27G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 3 minutes 33 seconds 0:02:56.137 1M / 27G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 2 minutes 56 seconds 0:03:44.267 1M / 28G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 3 minutes 44 seconds 0:12:29.298 1M / 28G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 12 minutes 29 seconds MI_OVERRIDE=OFF 0:03:06.738 0M / 13G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 3 minutes 6 seconds 0:02:35.686 0M / 15G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 2 minutes 35 seconds 0:03:32.876 0M / 14G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 3 minutes 32 seconds 0:10:33.023 0M / 14G INFO General (main.cpp : 136) Assembling time: 0 hours 10 minutes 33 seconds ``` It is possible this is only true on our system for the specific case we are looking at.

output: I have a similar problem as Fla1487 with two different MacBook Pro devices as well (Monterey 12.6.6; Ventura 13.4). Just putting my experience here in case it is helpful to Fla1487 or others who may encounter the same problem: When I use `curl http://cab.spbu.ru/files/release3.15.5/SPAdes-3.15.5-Darwin.tar.gz -o SPAdes-3.15.5-Darwin.tar.gz`I download a file but it is empty (0 bytes). When I force redirect using `curl -L http://cab.spbu.ru/files/release3.15.5/SPAdes-3.15.5-Darwin.tar.gz -o SPAdes-3.15.5-Darwin.tar.gz`, I get a 77bytes file, which is too small. I can download the file from my browser by going to http://cab.spbu.ru/files/release3.15.5/SPAdes-3.15.5-Darwin.tar.gz, after which I can install using `tar -zxf SPAdes-3.15.5-Darwin.tar.gz`, and got to the `bin` directory with all the files as expected (in fact I get more files than expected - additional files include cds-mapping-stats, cds-subgraphs, mag-improve, spades-convert-bin-to-fasta, spades-gsimplifier, spades-kmer-estimating, spades-read-filter, spades-read-filter, spades_init.py, spaligner). When I tried to run the test from the `bin` directory using `python3 ./spades.py --test` I couldn't complete the process as macOS couldn't verify that `spades-hammer` is free from malware and that the developer cannot be verified. It turns out that every single executable file has the same problem, so I did the low-tech method of right-clicking "open" from Finder and overriding macOS's security warning for all executable files before trying again. The test completed with warnings: ``` ======= SPAdes pipeline finished WITH WARNINGS! === Error correction and assembling warnings: * 0:00:00.571 1M / 154M WARN General (launcher.cpp : 178) Your data seems to have high uniform coverage depth. It is strongly recommended to use --isolate option. ``` which I don't understand since it was the test dataset... but at least the code ran. For some reason I can't run `spades.py --test` without including invoking `python3` first

input: ### Description of bug Error code as below. This worked no problem for my other 14 assemblies so I don't think it is to do with installation. Machine has 384 GB RAM so this shouldn't be the issue either. <jemalloc>: Error in malloc(): out of memory. Requested: 896, active: 266787094528 == Error == system call for: "['/home/pharmmicro/anaconda3/envs/spades/share/spades-3.13.0-0/bin/spades-core', '/home/pharmmicro/Documents/randox/contigs/sinkd/K55/configs/config.info', '/home/pharmmicro/Documents/randox/contigs/sinkd/K55/configs/mda_mode.info', '/home/pharmmicro/Documents/randox/contigs/sinkd/K55/configs/meta_mode.info']" finished abnormally, err code: -6 ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/12259421/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/12259423/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.13.0 ### Operating System Ubuntu 20.04.6 ### Python Version _No response_ ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: The log reads: ``` <jemalloc>: Error in malloc(): out of memory. Requested: 896, active: 266787094528 ``` So you're running out of RAM. It's perfectly ok to different samples have different memory requirements. I would probably suggest you to switch to the latest SPAdes 3.15.5.

instruction: Error [] call -6 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hi there, I am attempting to use SPAdes to assemble some metagenomic data. Here is my input: "spades --meta --pe1-1 004_1ee.fastq --pe1-2 004_2ee.fastq -k 55,77,99 -t 20 -m 150 -o 004_metaspades --phred-offset 33" I am including the phred offset because I was previously getting an error 255 flashed, and saw that phred-offset might fix it. I had done some other reading on error -6, and saw that it may happen because my input fastqs are two different sizes: this is not the case, they are the same size and contain the same number of reads. I even verified this with fastq_pair, which told me all my left and right reads were paired and produced no singles. It may be worth noting that this data was generated by running the extract_reads.py script from Jennifer Lu's "KrakenTools", as I was trying to remove all non-microbial reads from my dataset prior to further downstream processing. SPAdes has no issue assembling my raw files, only these extracted ones. Any insight or help you could give would be greatly appreciated- thank you! Best, Nolan Amon ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/11854273/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/11854274/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes 3.13.1 ### Operating System Ubuntu 22.04.2 ### Python Version Python 3.10.9 ### Method of SPAdes installation Manual ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: > Hi Anton, > > Thanks for your prompt reply! Here are the first few lines of my forward fastq: > > > A00589:519:HHJW5DMXY:2:1101:17879:1047 1:N:0:TATCCTCCAG+CATTGGTGCG > > GCGTTTTTTTATTTCTCCTGTAGGAGCTGCCGAAGGCTGCGAAAGCGGTGTTTCAGGTAA > > ATTGCTTTTTCAGCCTTCGTCAAACATAAAGTAACTAACTGAATTCAAATGCTTTATCTG > > TAGGAGCGCGCTTGCCCACGATTGCGTCACC > > A00589:519:HHJW5DMXY:2:1101:17770:1078 1:N:0:TATCCTCCAG+CATTGGTGCG > > GCGTTTTTTTATTTCTCCTGTAGGAGCTGCCGAAGGCTGCGAAAGCGGTGTTTCAGGTAA > > ATTTCTTTTTCAGCCTTCGTCAAACATAAAGTAACTAACTGAATTCAAATGCTTTATCTG > > TAGGAGCGCGCTTGCCCGCGATTGCGTCACC > > A00589:519:HHJW5DMXY:2:1101:20139:1078 1:N:0:TATCCTCCAG+CATTGGTGCG > > GCGTCGACCGCCAACAACCTGAAGAAGGACGGCTGGCAGACCGGCCAGAGCTGGGGCTAC > > GAGGTCTCGGTGCCGCAGGGTTTCAACTACATGCTGGCCGACCGCGCCAAGACCATGACT > > TTCGCGCAGTGGCAGCAGCTCGGCGTCGTGC > > If my reads are corrupted- is there any way to fix them? Thanks again! This is not a FASTQ file (and not a FASTA either). You'd probably need to ask the author of whatever script you used to fix it.

output: @EvolDoc, Also I checked your spades.log and not really sure that wastewaterSPAdes is what you need. I would suggest using coronaSPAdes if you want to assemble enteroviral genomes (maybe you will need to provide a non-standard set of HMMs using --custom-hmms options). wastewaterSPAdes works only with SARS-CoV-2, since it uses SARS-CoV-2 variant matrix to assign relative abundances to the strains and will not work with Enteroviruses

instruction: --assembly-graph parameter does not seem to work (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hello, I am trying to run metaviralspades using metaspades assembly assembly graph as a parameter, but with no success. This is my command: ``` spades.py--metaviral -t 100 -1 ZRH_6_tmp_1.fastq.gz -2 ZRH_6_tmp_2.fastq.gz --assembly_graph metaspades/K77/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa -k77 -o metaviralspades_on_metaspades_graph ``` And this is message I get: ``` SPAdes genome assembler v3.15.5 [metaviralSPAdes mode] Usage: spades.py [options] -o <output_dir> spades.py: error: Please specify option (e.g. -1, -2, -s, etc)) for the following paths: --assembly_graph, metaspades/K77/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa ``` Prior to that, I did a normal metaspades run: `spades.py -t 100 -1 ZRH_6_tmp_1.fastq.gz -2 ZRH_6_tmp_2.fastq.gz -o metaspades` And the files inside my standard metaspades run seem to be okay, including the scaffolds and the gfa files. ``` cd metaspades find . -name "*gfa" ./K77/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa ./K77/assembly_graph_after_simplification.gfa ./assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa ./assembly_graph_after_simplification.gfa find . -name "*scaffolds*" ./scaffolds.paths ./K77/scaffolds.paths ./K77/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa ./K77/scaffolds.fasta ./assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa ./scaffolds.fasta ./misc/broken_scaffolds.fasta ``` I believe I am providing the correct parameters. Could I please get some help with that? Has someone managed to run meta(viral)spades using .gfa file as parameter? Thank you! ### spades.log Is not generated. ### params.txt Is not generated. ### SPAdes version v3.15.5 ### Operating System Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS ### Python Version _No response_ ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [x] Yes
output: Hi Anton, thanks for the reply. It was a typo in my last command, but please see an updated version below: ``` spades.py --metaviral -t 100 -1 ZRH_6_tmp_1.fastq.gz -2 ZRH_6_tmp_2.fastq.gz --assembly-graph metaspades/K77/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa -k77 -o metaviralspades_on_metaspades_graph == Error == to run read error correction, reads should be in FASTQ format (.fq, .fastq, .bam, .fq.gz, .fastq.gz are supported): /storage/testing_tools/camp_viral/TESTING_TOOLS/metaspades/K77/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa (single reads, library number: 1, library type: assembly-graph) In case you have troubles running SPAdes, you can write to spades.support@cab.spbu.ru or report an issue on our GitHub repository github.com/ablab/spades ```

instruction: resume with more memory (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: By design you need just run with --checkpoint all and for restart run only spades only with --continue and -o. _Originally posted by @olga24912 in https://github.com/ablab/spades/issues/956#issuecomment-1076734404_ Dear Olga, I have an assembly that keeps failing due to memory (already gave it 500GB). Last time I used all checkpoints to be able to resume in case that happened again. I am trying to relaunch it using just flags --continue -o flags, and --memory 600, but it fails saying I cannot give it any flags other then --continue and -o. How can I restart with more memory without giving it the flag for memory?… Thanks in advance! Command: spades.py -t 16 --memory 600 --continue -o spades Log: Fri Apr 7 15:03:45 CDT 2023 == Error == you cannot specify any option except -o with --continue option! Please use '--restart-from last' if you need to change some of the options from the initial run and continue from the last available checkpoint.
output: The log clearly reads: ``` == Error == you cannot specify any option except -o with --continue option! Please use '--restart-from last' if you need to change some of the options from the initial run and continue from the last available checkpoint. ``` Have you tried to do as you were suggested? E.g. `spades.py -t 16 --memory 600 --restart-from last -o spades`?

Please help me to understand my error. ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/11075070/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/11075067/params.txt) ### SPAdes version v3.15.5 ### Operating System Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS ### Python Version Python 3.10.6 ### Method of SPAdes installation manual ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes

instruction: No complete extrachromosomal contigs assembled!! (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I tried spades for my viral genome assembly. After the end of the assembly, I just received "No complete extrachromosomal contigs assembled!!" in the log file. Also, I couldn't find any contigs or scaffold files. Does this message mean that there is no viral genome present in that particular sample? Thank you in advance ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10978584/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10978618/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.15.4 ### Operating System Linux-4.12.14-197.40-default-x86_64-with-glibc2.26 ### Python Version 3.10.9 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: > Hi No, this means that for some reasons we were not able to assemble _complete_ viral genomes - metaviralSPAdes is focused on them only. Usual problems are coverage gaps or ultra-high coverage, but other issues can appear too. > > You can just try regular (meta) SPAdes to get the complete assembly and extract viral fragments from it. Hi, Ive tried metaspade and with similar sample and I got 2047 contigs and I tried filtering out based on virVerify and checkV. Got good quality contigs. Thank you

input: ### Description of bug Hello, I want to use SPAdes to assemble Several Illumina bacterial sequencing data at the same time, the command I use is as follows: python spades.py --isolate --pe1-1 /home/dell/spades/projects/demo/058_1.clean.fq.gz --pe1-2 /home/dell/spades/projects/demo/058_2.clean.fq.gz \ > --pe2-1 /home/dell/spades/projects/demo/059_1.clean.fq.gz --pe2-2 /home/dell/spades/projects/demo/059_2.clean.fq.gz \ > -o /home/dell/spades/projects/demo/output I input the sequencing data for 2 strains, but in the output folder, only one scaffolds.fasta is shown. i would like to know what went wrong, thank you ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10809936/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10809938/params.txt) ### SPAdes version v3.15.5 ### Operating System CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core) ### Python Version Python 3.9.13 ### Method of SPAdes installation binaries ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Nothing is wrong. You need to run SPAdes for your data separately for different samples

instruction: Small viral contigs in denovo assembly using spades (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Dear All, I am trying spades to perform de novo assembly for my viral genomes. Unfortunately, these viral genomes have no references to compare with. For some of the samples, I got 40kb to 90kp contigs. But for some, I get only 3000 bp to 5000 bp contigs. What could be the reason (both technical and biological reasons) behind this? If this is due to technical issues, how do I improve the assembly to get a contig with more kb? I also check the log file but couldn't find anything alarming Thank you in advance Regards Monica ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10791559/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10791570/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.15.4 ### Operating System Linux-4.12.14-197.40-default-x86_64-with-glibc2.26 ### Python Version 3.10.9 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Hi, There is no way to "extend" such contigs, they represent something that can be a virus due to the graph structure and coverage. Some viruses have really short genomes, even shorter than 3kb However, that's quite possible that these contigs are just false positives, and represent some repeat or something - we recommend to use some tool (viralVerify, virFinder) to check metaviralSPAdes output and remove false positives.

instruction: ERROR: Too many erroneous kmers, the estimates might be unreliable (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hello, I have a looked up a few other posts about this error but still unsure how to correct it. Even though the spades.log says `SPAdes pipeline finished WITH WARNINGS!` a fasta file is not generated. After look at the get_org.log file, it looks like there was an error in slimming. So then I looked into the slim.log and it says `ERROR: BLAST Database error: No alias or index file found for nucleotide database [/home/jhallas/.GetOrganelle/LabelDatabase/animal_mt] in search path [/share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/scripts::]`. I assembled 79 other genomes and this is the only one that encountered this error. I am using a circular mtDNA genome from the same species as the seed. My sequencing coverage is 25X. ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10540566/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/10540572/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes version: 3.13.0 ### Operating System OS: Linux-4.15.0-142-generic-x86_64-with-glibc2.17 ### Python Version Python version: 3.8.15 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: SPAdes finished successfully: ``` * Corrected reads are in /share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/mtDNA/getorganelle/DB10020/extended_spades/corrected/ * Assembled contigs are in /share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/mtDNA/getorganelle/DB10020/extended_spades/contigs.fasta * Assembled scaffolds are in /share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/mtDNA/getorganelle/DB10020/extended_spades/scaffolds.fasta * Assembly graph is in /share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/mtDNA/getorganelle/DB10020/extended_spades/assembly_graph.fastg * Assembly graph in GFA format is in /share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/mtDNA/getorganelle/DB10020/extended_spades/assembly_graph_with_scaffolds.gfa * Paths in the assembly graph corresponding to the contigs are in /share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/mtDNA/getorganelle/DB10020/extended_spades/contigs.paths * Paths in the assembly graph corresponding to the scaffolds are in /share/cdfwwildlife/hallas_dedicated/lc_deer/mtDNA/getorganelle/DB10020/extended_spades/scaffolds.paths ``` SPAdes does not use BLAST and this is not from SPAdes log. Likely you're using some wrapper / pipeline, so the problem is not with SPAdes, I would suggest you to report your problem elsewhere

instruction: Assemble of DNA and RNA amplicons from PacBio data (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. For generic questions use Q&A section in the Discussions forum above. Dear Mr/Mrs, I am trying to combine data from two PacBio runs: DNA (10kb) + cDNA (10kb), these regions overlap each other partially and I would like to phase them based on short-tandem repeats and exonic SNP/fingerprint to genomic exon+intron SNP/fingerprints to identify intronic SNP and a number of tandem repeats that are separated by >150kb from each other. cDNA was used to bring STR's + exonic SNP closer to each other. Do you think SPADES could assemble DNA (intronic and exonic) + RNA/cDNA reads (exonic) into a graph where we could: phase the number of STR's + exonic SNP (cDNA) with intronic and exonic fingerprints (DNA) based on 99-100% similarity? If yes, which of the packages I should use, if not do you have any idea what software I should consider using? Thank you very much and please let me know if you have any questions. S-T-C ### Describe the solution you'd like Apply 99-100% similarity to assemble DNA and cDNA data based on fingerprints and maintain the SNP information to avoid data collapsing and fake results. ### Describe alternatives you've considered UCLUST or VSEARCH for OTU binning ### Additional context _No response_
output: Hello SPAdes is designed and intended to be used on Illumina-provided data. It cannot be used on PacBio data alone. You could try other software packages, e.g. miniasm, etc.

instruction: Assembling of multiple metatranscriptomics samples together? #1237 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Hello, I would like to co-assemble multiple metatranscriptomics samples together. Or giving multiple samples as input instead of one. Would it be possible to do it with rnaSPAdes? Thank you. Kind regards, Jaspreet
output: Dear Jaspreet Yes, I suggest to provide all samples as a sinlge library (if the were sequenced using the same protocol and have e.g. the same insert size) using --pe1-1 --pe1-2 options. This way you'll most likely get the most complete metatranscriptome assembly. You can find some examples here: https://github.com/ablab/spades#examples If you are interested in quantification, I suggest to further map reads back to assembled transcripts to estimate their per-sample abundances. Best Andrey
