input: ### Description of bug Hi， I found the error, but I don't know how to solve it. Can you give me some advice? In the first run, I thought there was something wrong with the file, and I used fastp to filter it, but the second run still had the same problem. ### spades.log 0:00:19.550 1M / 72M ERROR General (kmer_coverage_model.cpp : 187) Invalid kmer coverage histogram, make sure that the coverage is indeed uniform == Error == system call for: "['/Bioinfo/home/liwei/miniconda3/envs/ttt/bin/spades-core', '/Bioinfo/home/liwei/project/HRN-2022041C01.Fz/flow/tmp/getorganelle/test2/result/check/1/animal_mt.check.fq.spades/K45/configs/config.info']" finished abnormally, OS return value: 21 ### params.txt Command line: /Bioinfo/home/liwei/miniconda3/envs/ttt/bin/spades.py -t 30 -s /Bioinfo/home/liwei/project/HRN-2022041C01.Fz/flow/tmp/getorganelle/test2/result/check/1/animal_mt.check.fq.more.fq -k 45 --only-assembler -o /Bioinfo/home/liwei/project/HRN-2022041C01.Fz/flow/tmp/getorganelle/test2/result/check/1/animal_mt.check.fq.spades System information: SPAdes version: 3.15.5 Python version: 3.10.8 OS: Linux-3.10.0-693.el7.x86_64-x86_64-with-glibc2.17 Output dir: /Bioinfo/home/liwei/project/HRN-2022041C01.Fz/flow/tmp/getorganelle/test2/result/check/1/animal_mt.check.fq.spades Mode: ONLY assembling (without read error correction) Debug mode is turned OFF Dataset parameters: Standard mode For multi-cell/isolate data we recommend to use '--isolate' option; for single-cell MDA data use '--sc'; for metagenomic data use '--meta'; for RNA-Seq use '--rna'. Reads: Library number: 1, library type: single left reads: not specified right reads: not specified interlaced reads: not specified single reads: ['/Bioinfo/home/liwei/project/HRN-2022041C01.Fz/flow/tmp/getorganelle/test2/result/check/1/animal_mt.check.fq.more.fq'] merged reads: not specified Assembly parameters: k: [45] Repeat resolution is enabled Mismatch careful mode is turned OFF MismatchCorrector will be SKIPPED Coverage cuto
output: So, from the log: ``` 0:00:02.826 2M / 43M INFO General (kmer_splitters.hpp : 131) Used 70 reads ``` You're having only 70 reads. This cannot be any meaningful part of any genome with even coverage (since you're assembling in normal multicell mode that expects decent part of a genome with even coverage), hence the error.

instruction: Highly improbable pattern in subset of samples failing to assemble (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hi, 6/54 of my samples didn't assemble using metaSpades, producing the same error as https://github.com/ablab/spades/issues/722 The reason I am opening a different issue is twofold. To begin, I found that ordering my 54 samples by unique kmers counts (from the first steps of K-mer counting) makes the 6 problematic samples contiguous. I had friends better at probabilites than I am help me figure out that the odds of any 6 items picked from an ordered set of 54 at random being contiguous by chance are in the order of 2e-6. So that's the first reason I'm thinking this might be worth reporting, in case this could point to a pattern. Visually: ![Bildschirmfoto 2022-10-22 um 11 01 20](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/97983060/197346383-dfe87082-ddc6-4c6d-91eb-4700266dcfc7.png) Small odds aside, I also tried splitting one of the problematic samples in two. Both halves went through the same assembly command without error. For now, this will be my workaround, but I am worried about the impact assembling two halves of a metagenomes seperately will have on the subsequent co-binning of these two outputs. I don't think it has anything to do with memory, as peak RAM usage is usually around 20-30% of available ram depending on the sample. In any case, I really hope this helps. Looking forward to hear from you on this! ### spades.log For the problematic (non-subset) sample : [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/9862243/spades.log) For one of the two subsets from the same sample : [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/9862254/spades.log) ### params.txt Same params file for both (except the sample name): [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/9862256/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.15.4 ### Operating System Linux-3.10.0-1160.76.1.el7.x86_64-x86_64-with-debian-bullseye-sid ### Python Version 2.7.15 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Highly likely the crash is in CQF implementation (https://github.com/splatlab/cqf/) we are using. Unfortunately, the only workaround is to skip the read error correction via (`--only-assembler`). You might want to perform quality trimming before this

input: ### Description of bug I just ran Spades for kmer sizes from 31,41,51,... to 111. In my output folder for the whole run, I can find one contigs.fasta file for the best assembly. instruction: Abnormal results using --trusted-contig (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug We made three hybrid assemblies with spades using different parameters for each. One with --nanopore, one with --trusted-contigs and one with --untrusted-contigs. When comparing the resulting assemblies, the ones using nanopore and untrusted contigs have similar values for N50, genome size, L50, etc. But the results for trusted contigs are drastically worse. We wonder if this could be explained logically or is likely due to an error in the commands used? ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/9446147/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/9446151/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.13.0 ### Operating System Linux-4.12.14-197.40-default-x86_64-with-glibc2.26 ### Python Version 3.10.4 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes

instruction: Can't submit metaSPAdes_assemble script using ${PBS_ARRAYID} when having the my_data_set.yaml file? (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. For generic questions use Q&A section in the Discussions forum above. Dear metaSPAdes support team, I am now assembling 9 paired-end samples using command line below. But, as my sample id is inconsistent, eg., 25,27, 29, 31-36. So, I use the **.yaml** file as the input of --dataset. But, if I write the absolute path of paired-end .fq.gz files, then I can't use the array id in my PBS job setting. For example, I can specify setting like, **PBS -t 25,27,29,31-36%3** or ${PBS_ARRAYID}. Could you give some suggestions on my question? **Command line** /services/tools/SPAdes/3.15.3/bin/spades.py --meta -k 21,33,55,77 --dataset /PATH_TO_YAML_FILE/my_data_set.yaml -t 40 -m 1500 -o /PATH_TO_OUPUT/spades_output_25_36 **--dataset - my_data_set.yaml.txt** [my_data_set copy.yaml.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/8837844/my_data_set.copy.yaml.txt) Best, Bing ### Describe the solution you'd like _No response_ ### Describe alternatives you've considered _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_
output: Thank you very much for replying me. My question is that I can't directly run the command line with **${PBS_ARRAYID}** as below due to I am using the **"my_data_set.yaml"** dataset file. The command line with **${PBS_ARRAYID}** /services/tools/SPAdes/3.15.3/bin/spades.py --meta -k 21,33,55,77 --pe${PBS_ARRAYID}-1 Sample${PBS_ARRAYID}_TM_paired_1.fastq --pe${PBS_ARRAYID}-2 Sample${PBS_ARRAYID}_TM_paired_2.fastq -t 32 -o ./Current_pipeline/metaSPAdes_assemble/Sample${PBS_ARRAYID} The command line with **"my_data_set.yaml"** dataset file, which I am running now. /services/tools/SPAdes/3.15.3/bin/spades.py --meta -k 21,33,55,77 --dataset /home/projects/env_10000/people/binson/MTB/assemble/metaSPAdes_assemble/my_data_set.yaml -t 40 -o /home/projects/env_10000/people/binson/MTB/assemble/metaSPAdes_assemble/spades_output_25_36 Best, Bing

input: ### Description of bug I ran LegioCluster which uses SPAdes and it generated an issue: ``` == Running read error correction tool: /SPAdes-3.12.0-Linux/bin/spades-hammer /data/SPAdes/corrected/configs/config.info The program was terminated by segmentation fault === Stack Trace === == Error == system call for: "['/SPAdes-3.12.0-Linux/bin/spades-hammer', '/data/SPAdes/corrected/configs/config.info']" finished abnormally, err code: -11 In case you have troubles running SPAdes, you can write to spades.support@cab.spbu.ru or report an issue on our GitHub repository github.com/ablab/spades Please provide us with params.txt and spades.log files from the output directory. ATTENTION !!! ('KI', 'Spy', 'Spy_sample_2', '/home/karimitani/lp_proj/LegioCluster/reads/Spy/Spy_sample_2_R1_001.fastq.gz', '/home/karimitani/lp_proj/LegioCluster/reads/Spy/Spy_sample_2_R2_001.fastq.gz', ['tutorial_part_2', 'multiple_submissions', 'normal_operations']) DID NOT COMPLETE SUCCESSFULLY: Command 'docker run --rm=True -u $(id -u):$(id -g) -v "/home/karimitani/lp_proj/LegioCluster/temp/KI220503_123322/:/data" -i staphb/spades:3.12.0 spades.py -1 paired_reads_1.fq -2 paired_reads_2.fq -k 21,33,55,77 --careful --cov-cutoff auto -o SPAdes' returned non-zero exit status 1. ``` ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/8612311/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/8612312/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes v3.12.0 ### Operating System Windows 11 64-bit ### Python Version Python 3.8.10 ### Method of SPAdes installation Through legiocluster ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: From SPAdes manual (https://cab.spbu.ru/files/release3.15.4/manual.html#sec2): ``` SPAdes requires a 64-bit Linux system or Mac OS and Python (supported versions are Python 2.7, and Python3: 3.2 and higher) ``` You are running SPAdes on Windows under WSL which is not quite Linux and the platform is not supported. Pre-built binaries are known not to work there. Others reported some success with SPAdes built from source, so I'd suggest you to take the latest SPAdes and compile it from source.

instruction: Adding spades to `PATH`: needs to append `PATH` with initial download location. (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug In order to make spades available from anywhere, I have to append the initial directory where spades is extracted to `$PATH`. Rather than being able to either symlink or copy the folder into a location which is already on the `PATH`. This is extremely surprising. I would prefer to symlink the `bin` folder to `\usr\local\bin` as this is my generic workflow for user installed software. ### SPAdes version 3.15.4 ### Operating System Ubuntu 20.04 ### Python Version 3.8.10 ### Method of SPAdes installation binaries ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Sorry @asl , what I'm saying is, symlinking/copying doesn't appear to work. I just tried to symlink the whole `SPAdes-3.15.4-Linux` folder to `/usr/local/bin` in order to make the `share` directory available too. Unfortunately my system still cant find `spades.py`. When I run `spades.py --test` I receive `bash: spades.py: command not found`. I have confirmed that it works when inside the `bin` directory and running `.\spades.py` and when I append `bin` to the `PATH` .

instruction: Update REDME to detail which Linux kernels the pre-build binaries will work for. (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. For generic questions use Q&A section in the Discussions forum above. I do not know if the pre-build binaries will work on my Linux system. "New kernels" is too vague. ### Describe the solution you'd like An update to the README with more details on which versions of the Linux kernel the pre-build binaries will work for. ### Additional context _No response_
output: Unfortunately we do not have bandwidth to test on all plethora of Linux kernel versions and distributions around. We are relying on reports of our users. So, I'd simply suggest you to download the binaries and run "spades.py --test". Patches are always welcome though :)

My error file is also empty. My command line: /services/tools/SPAdes/3.15.3/bin/spades.py --meta --pe2-1 Sample2_TM_paired_1.fastq --pe2-2 Sample2_TM_paired_2.fastq --pe3-1 Sample3_TM_paired_1.fastq --pe3-2 Sample3_TM_paired_2.fastq --pe4-1 Sample4_TM_paired_1.fastq --pe4-2 Sample4_TM_paired_2.fastq --pe5-1 Sample5_TM_paired_1.fastq --pe5-2 Sample5_TM_paired_2.fastq --pe6-1 Sample6_TM_paired_1.fastq --pe6-2 Sample6_TM_paired_2.fastq --pe7-1 Sample7_TM_paired_1.fastq --pe7-2 Sample7_TM_paired_2.fastq -k 21,33,55,77 -t 24 -o ./Current_pipeline/Individual_assembly/ Could you help me have a look what is wrong there? Best, Bing ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/8501865/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/8501867/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes v3.15.3 ### Operating System Computerome 2.0, DTU ### Python Version Python 3.6.10 ### Method of SPAdes installation conda ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes

Reading https://currentprotocols.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1002/cpbi.102 might also help

instruction: OS return value: -6 in version 3.15.4 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug We are trying to assemble a verterbrate genome in a cluster (251Gb, 64 cores). It seems it is asking for too much memory or some step fails before the end. We noticed there are similar issues but it appeared as fixed in version 3.15.2. We launched the job with a simple `nohup ./SPADES/SPAdes-3.15.4-Linux/bin/spades.py --pe1-1 /Trim_het/mahali_R1_trimPair.fastq --pe1-2 /Trim_het/mahali_R2_trimPair.fastq --isolate --threads 30 -m 230 -o OUT &` > 4:11:00.950 118G / 165G INFO General (debruijn_graph_constructor: 487) Total 5493423152 edges to create > mimalloc: error: unable to allocate OS memory (220842688512 bytes, error code: 12 [Cannot allocate memory], address: (nil), large only: 0, allow large: 1) Thanks for your help Andrés ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/8327606/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/8327618/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes-3.15.4-Linux ### Operating System ID_LIKE="fedora" VERSION_ID="8.5" ### Python Version 3.8.12 ### Method of SPAdes installation binaries ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Well, it is running out of RAM. You need more RAM to assemble your very large dataset.

instruction: Issue in test suite of metaspades (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug metaspades.py --test leads to segmentation fault. I tried to track down the issue via gdb and have added some debug lines to do so. Please check this [debug code](https://salsa.debian.org/med-team/spades/-/blob/master/debian/patches/debug.patch) I injected into the Debian packaging. It seems `last_word` becomes zero and than you want to address something at `last_word - 1`. Hope this helps. Kind regards Andreas. ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/7495735/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/7495737/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.15.3 ### Operating System Debian (unstable) ### Python Version Python 3.9.2 ### Method of SPAdes installation Debian package ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Hi Anton, I have been looking at this problem today, and discovered it is related to a change I introduced in _gqf_ to fix a problem of architecture baseline violation more than a year ago which turns out to be the culprit. I replaced the inline assembly call by a generic ___builtin_popcount_, but did not properly take into account the full width of the _uint64_t_ argument, leading to an overflow of the value and thus an improper result of all _popcnt_ function calls. Using ___builtin_popcountl_, note the trainling _l_, fixes the overflow and allows me to run the test suite properly. In case you are interested to provide Spades support to CPU generations older than those which support SSE4, please feel free to make use of the following patch, which didn't raise the present issue on my side: ```patch --- spades.orig/assembler/ext/src/gqf/gqf.c +++ spades/assembler/ext/src/gqf/gqf.c @@ -174,11 +174,7 @@ static inline int popcnt(uint64_t val) { - asm("popcnt %[val], %[val]" - : [val] "+r" (val) - : - : "cc"); - return val; + return __builtin_popcountl(val); } static inline int popcntv(const uint64_t val, int ignore) ``` There is no problem in your code, it's all my fault. :( I think you can close the issue. With my sincerest apologies, Étienne.

instruction: Upgrade pyyaml to support Python 3.10 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. For generic questions use Q&A section in the Discussions forum above. `pyyaml` is currently at version `3.10` while the [latest is currently 6.0](https://pypi.org/project/PyYAML/#history). Python 3.10 includes the deprecation of classes that are generic within `collections`. Currently, running Spades with Python 3.10 leads to the error ``` AttributeError: module 'collections' has no attribute 'Hashable' ``` ### Describe the solution you'd like The issue [has been fixed in 2019](https://github.com/yaml/pyyaml/commit/9959328b41ee577c6f14e3a0184b07dd0ecadb8c) in `pyyaml` so the fix would simply be to upgrade it. ### Describe alternatives you've considered You could always change the readme to state that Spades doesn't support Python >= 3.10. ### Additional context _No response_
output: Thanks @SchwarzEM > I just came across this bug report. Thanks to @Moisan and @SilasK for posting about it, because it allowed me to fix what had been a complete show-stopper problem. I fixed it by setting up a python_3.9 environment in conda and then running the precompiled Linux binaries within that activated environment. Normally I use bioconda to install software; the current bioconda package for the most recent versions of SPAdes have python 3.10 associated with them, and so cannot be run. When it is possible, I hope pyyaml can be updated in both the free-standing and bioconda-provided versions of SPAdes. This worked charmly for me. At least, the code is running. We'll check the output later. The steps were as follows (for anyone out there who needs this) - Create a conda environment with Pyhton 3.9 `$ conda create -n spadesenv python=3.9` `$ conda activate spadesenv` - Install the binary for Linux [(see here)](https://github.com/ablab/spades#downloading-spades-linux-binaries) `$ wget http://cab.spbu.ru/files/release3.15.5/SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux.tar.gz` `$ tar -xzf SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux.tar.gz` `$ cd SPAdes-3.15.5-Linux/bin/` - Run the command `$ metaspades.py -1 file_R1.fastq.gz -2 file_R2.fastq.gz --threads 24 -o outdir/`

instruction: Crash with --careful (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I'm running spades within geneious, and it was crashing, whereas when I ran it manually in Ubuntu it completed. Further investigation showed that it's the '--careful' parameter that's causing problems, but only on files that I have run the deduplication process on in geneious. I.e. on my original data, spades completes with or without --careful. On deduplicated data, it only completes if I leave --careful out of the command line. The error I get is: == Error == system call for: "['/usr/lib/spades/bin/spades-corrector-core', '/home/rich/3158-spades/mismatch_corrector/contigs/configs/corrector.info', '/home/rich/3158-spades/misc/assembled_contigs.fasta']" finished abnormally, err code: 255 ======= SPAdes pipeline finished abnormally and WITH WARNINGS! === Error correction and assembling warnings: * 0:00:07.067 72M / 3G WARN General (simplification.cpp : 479) The determined erroneous connection coverage threshold may be determined improperly ======= Warnings saved to /home/rich/3158-spades/warnings.log === ERRORs: * 0:00:04.185 4M / 4M ERROR DatasetProcessor (dataset_processor.cpp : 215) Failed to align paired reads /home/rich/3158-1.fastq and /home/rich/3158-2.fastq * system call for: "['/usr/lib/spades/bin/spades-corrector-core', '/home/rich/3158-spades/mismatch_corrector/contigs/configs/corrector.info', '/home/rich/3158-spades/misc/assembled_contigs.fasta']" finished abnormally, err code: 255 Any ideas? ### spades.log Command line: /usr/lib/spades/bin/spades.py --careful -1 /home/rich/3158-1.fastq -2 /home/rich/3158-2.fastq -o /home/rich/3158-spades System information: SPAdes version: 3.13.1 Python version: 3.9.5 OS: Linux-5.11.0-31-generic-x86_64-with-glibc2.33 Output dir: /home/rich/3158-spades Mode: read error correction and assembling Debug mode is turned OFF Dataset parameters: Multi-cell mode (you should set '--sc' flag if input data was obtained with MDA (single-cell) technology or --meta flag if processing metagenomic dataset) Reads: L
output: Ok, let's escalate the things little bit. Geneious is clearly screwing the read names and this is what bwa is complaining about. The original read names likely were (and you could check this on your original data): ``` NB501042:283:H7LVFAFX2:1:11101:2520:1139 1:N:0:4 NB501042:283:H7LVFAFX2:1:11101:2520:1139 2:N:0:4 ``` However, these are not *read names*. If Geneous folks would read the FASTQ specification, then they will be able to find out that the space symbol is used to start a comment in FASTQ record header. So, before their transformation read names were identical: `NB501042:283:H7LVFAFX2:1:11101:2520:1139` and `1:N:0:4` and `2:N:0:4` were comments. Now, after their transformation we obtained unequal read names and this is really something that they need to fix (certainly they will insist on doing anything, so you'd need to be prepared that you won't receive any support for your paid Geneious license). So, as you noted – SPAdes in `--careful` mode works fine with original reads and therefore Geneious really needs to fix how they treat the read names ;) Additionally, I'd need to note, that this is not SPAdes itself that errors out, but bwa is. So, if you'd try to align these reads with bwa in paired-end mode, then you will obtain the same error.

instruction: setrlimit(2) call failed, errno = 22 on m1 Mac using spades.py --test (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug Hi I have a M1 Mac, and spades is failing even on the base test installation process, the error I'm getting is "setrlimit(2) call failed, errno = 22" Thanks Ben ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/7096136/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/7096137/params.txt) ### SPAdes version SPAdes-3.15.3-Darwin ### Operating System MacOs 12.0 Beta (21A5304g) ### Python Version 3.8.2 ### Method of SPAdes installation binary ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: > Mac M1 chip user here. System is Monterey. Specifying -m 1024 works, but based on the manual, that's 1024 Gb of memory. Maybe the units are off? Units are not off. Note that this is not *memory*, but *memory bar* that SPAdes will never overshoot. It seems one cannot set memory limit on Monterey anymore, setting it high enough makes the error disappear (lower values yields `error` from OS). This is why we said it is broken... 3.15.4 will introduce workaround for this and it won't be hard error anymore, just warning that user is on their own.

instruction: SPAdes 3.15.3 does not catch linux binary run via WSL2 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug @asl informed me that SPAdes 3.15.3 checks for running linux binaries run via WSL2 and returns a sensible error message. My quick search through commit history points me here: https://github.com/ablab/spades/commit/0872b68c93367c0699c01b985b5e247a59d96b52 This was news, to me, so I gave it a quick try on WSL2 using a docker container for SPAdes 3.15.3 that I developed, using the SPAdes linux binary. But I encountered a non-intuitive error message and not the error message in the commit linked above. I think I hit an edge case? Dockerfile here: https://github.com/StaPH-B/docker-builds/blob/master/spades/3.15.3/Dockerfile dockerhub repo here: https://hub.docker.com/r/staphb/spades Ran this via WSL2 with Ubuntu 20.04.3 installed via Microsoft Store. (this is not reflected in the spades.log. Not sure why, but perhaps because I've upgraded from Ubuntu 16->18->20 on WSL2) Happy to provide more info if needed. To reproduce: ``` $ docker pull staphb/spades:3.15.3 $ docker run --rm -ti staphb/spades:3.15.3 # spades.py --test ``` ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/7093393/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/7093397/params.txt) ### SPAdes version 3.15.3 ### Operating System WSL2, Ubuntu 18 ### Python Version 2.7.12 (in container) ### Method of SPAdes installation docker container with SPAdes linux binary ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: ah! Good catch. That solved the issue. My docker container needs to be updated to include python3 ! I switched the docker container to use `ubuntu:bionic` as a base image, installed `python3` (3.6.9) & `python3-distutils` via `apt` then tried test again. This time I got the helpful error message. ``` # spades.py --test == Warning == No assembly mode was specified! If you intend to assemble high-coverage multi-cell/isolate data, use '--isolate' option. Command line: bin/spades.py --test System information: SPAdes version: 3.15.3 Python version: 3.6.9 OS: Linux-5.10.16.3-microsoft-standard-WSL2-x86_64-with-Ubuntu-18.04-bionic Output dir: /SPAdes-3.15.3-Linux/spades_test Mode: read error correction and assembling Debug mode is turned OFF Dataset parameters: Standard mode For multi-cell/isolate data we recommend to use '--isolate' option; for single-cell MDA data use '--sc'; for metagenomic data use '--meta'; for RNA-Seq use '--rna'. Log can be found here: CYP2C19_15_spades/spades.log == Splitting CYP2C19_15.001_final.fastq into left and right reads (in CYP2C19_15_spades/split_input directory) ===== Read error correction started. == Running read error correction tool: /opt/exp_soft/bioinf/SPAdes-3.13.0-Linux/bin/spades-hammer CYP2C19_15_spades/corrected/configs/config.info 0:00:00.000 4M / 4M INFO General (main.cpp : 75) Starting BayesHammer, buil
output: The error is clearly seen in the log file. Let us check it together: ``` 0:00:00.005 4M / 4M INFO General (main.cpp : 86) Trying to determine PHRED offset 0:00:00.059 4M / 4M ERROR General (main.cpp : 89) Failed to determine offset! Specify it manually and restart, please! ``` And indeed, your files are unlikely a valid FASTQ as all quality values are exactly the same. The read error correction relies on quality values and therefore certainly cannot proceed.

instruction: metaviralSPAdes keeping ony large contigs (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Not an issue anymore. Just a question, is there a length filtering in the contigs.fasta file for metaviralspades?
output: Yes. We have both straighforward (do not output anything shorter than 1000bp) and not straightforward filtering: do not output contigs that represent only fragments of viruses due to the assembly graph structure.

instruction: Spades multithreading is not working (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: ### Description of bug I am running a program which utilizes Spades as the assembler, I have noted that during the assembly step spades will default to using 1 thread no matter how many I have designated with the -t option. I also got an email from the sever admin saying that the program wasn't utilising all of the CPU space I had reserved. ``` 1:49:40.847 1M / 29M INFO General (kmer_index_builder.hpp : 243) Splitting kmer instances into 10 files using 1 threads. This might take a while. ``` ### spades.log [spades.log](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/6775782/spades.log) ### params.txt [params.txt](https://github.com/ablab/spades/files/6775790/params.txt) ### SPAdes version v3.15.2 ### Operating System CentOS Linux v8.4.2105 ### Python Version Python 3.9.2 ### Method of SPAdes installation From Squeezemeta source code ### No errors reported in spades.log - [X] Yes
output: Hello The log reads: ``` 0:00:00.004 1M / 14M INFO General (main.cpp : 110) Maximum # of threads to use (adjusted due to OMP capabilities): 1 ``` So, you'd likely need to do something additional in order to enable multiple CPU threads for OpenMP tasks. Likely your system administrator could tell you necessary additional configuration steps. On many computation clusters you'd need to do something like this: ``` export OMP_NUM_THREADS=<number of threads allowed> ```

instruction: How many transcript isoforms does rnaSPAdes report? (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Hi, Thank you for the great tool!! I'm looking at the output from a `rnaSPAdes` _de novo_ assembly (short reads), and I see that the contigs are identified in a style similar to that used by the `Trinity` _de novo_ assembler. I.e., `somecontig_somegene_someisoform`. It appears that in some cases `rnaSPAdes` has resolved as many as 54 isoforms. However, in the output, for each `NODE_x_length_x_cov_x_gx` combo, only a single isoform is in the output (e.g., just `NODE_1234_length_1234_cov_100.00_g123_i12`). **My question is: where are the rest of the isoforms?** I seem to have only one isoform per gene, for all genes, and all assemblies that I attempted. I am looking at the `transcripts.fasta` file, by the way. `SPAdes` version is `v3.15.2`.
output: @andrewprzh , Thank you for looking into this, but I'm afraid i don't follow. I guess I am confused, so let me clarify. If I were to take a `Trinity` assembly, and run it through a `bash` one-liner to count the number of isoforms per "gene", I'd get something like this: ``` $ grep -oP "(?<=^\>)[A-Za-z0-9\._]+(?=_i[0-9]+)" Trinity.Trinity.fasta | sort | uniq -c | sort -n -k 1 | tail 28 TRINITY_DN98_c1_g1 29 TRINITY_DN1196_c0_g1 29 TRINITY_DN522_c0_g1 29 TRINITY_DN585_c0_g1 32 TRINITY_DN599_c0_g1 32 TRINITY_DN738_c0_g1 33 TRINITY_DN268_c2_g1 34 TRINITY_DN33_c0_g1 38 TRINITY_DN361_c0_g1 50 TRINITY_DN98_c5_g1 ``` For instance, `TRINITY_DN98_c5_g1` has 50 different isoforms (`TRINITY_DN98_c5_g1_iX`) present in the file. When I run a `rnaSPAdes` assembly through the same one-liner, I get something like this: ``` $ grep -oP "(?<=^\>)[A-Za-z0-9\._]+(?=_i[0-9]+)" transcripts.fasta | sort | uniq -c | sort -n -k 1 | tail 1 NODE_9993_length_1212_cov_5.397612_g7722 1 NODE_9994_length_1212_cov_5.310376_g7723 1 NODE_9995_length_1212_cov_3.889807_g7724 1 NODE_9996_length_1212_cov_2.663912_g7725 1 NODE_9997_length_1212_cov_1.393939_g7726 1 NODE_9998_length_1211_cov_45.217831_g7727 1 NODE_9999_length_1211_cov_34.731618_g7727 1 NODE_999_length_3607_cov_9.287887_g639 1 NODE_99_length_6978_cov_41.546317_g57 1 NODE_9_length_11858_cov_73.766170_g1 ``` It is true that the `rnaSPAdes` assembly does have sequences with headers like `>NODE_363_length_4843_cov_2549.987288_g3_i18` (indicating there are/were 18 isoforms), but when I `grep` for that gene (i.e., `>NODE_363_length_4843_cov_2549.987288_g3`), I only find that one sequence in the assembly and nothing else. Hence my question: where are the other isoforms?

instruction: Format of contigs.seq (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Once a genome assembly is completed, in some lower k's folders, for example K21 folder, there is a subfolder 'simplified contigs'. This contains a contigs.seq file. This does not appear to be text format file as would be expected. What exactly is the file's format? Can this be converted into a fasta format?
output: Right, these are SPAdes internal files and are not intended for estimation of "stagewise quality of assemblies at each k", this is something that does not exist in SPAdes world. SPAdes uses all information from multiple k-mer lengths to construct a final assembly, it's not doing merely "choose best result from multiple k-mers" as other assembly approaches do.

instruction: Metaplasmidspades contigs that are NOT categorized as "plasmid" (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Dear developers, I'm comparing the plasmid prediction of Metaplasmidspades for known plasmid datasets and would like to calculate True negative(TN) and false negatives for the dataset. Can we reach the contigs that Metaplasmidspades categorized as "non-plasmid"? Thanks in advance.
output: Hi. Metaplasmidspades is not a plasmid classification tool. Plasmid assembly is improved with iterative removal of non-plasmid edges of the assembly graph. So we do not output non-plasmid sequences (not contigs, these sequences are removed before contig generation). For the plasmid/nonplasmid classification I can suggest to use viralVerify ( https://github.com/ablab/viralVerify ) - it classifies contigs of viral, chromosomal and plasmid origin based on gene content.

Likely you have not added SPAdes installation folder in your PATH and / or simply starting it incorrectly. In this case some basic Linux cmdline resources might provide necessary help

instruction: metaSPAdes error with err code 255 (Tool: MetaSPAdes)
input: Hey, recently I used SPAdes to assembly a metagenome, but it always show me the error, even if I grow the threads to 64 and the memory limits to 500 Gb. Could you help me deal with this problem? The log and txt are below. **spades.log** `Command line: /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/miniconda3/envs/nanopore/bin/spades.py -m 500 -t 64 -1 /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/JHW1_BDME200006960-1a_1.fq.gz -2 /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/JHW1_BDME200006960-1a_2.fq.gz -o /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/output --meta System information: SPAdes version: 3.13.0 Python version: 3.6.12 OS: Linux-3.10.0-1062.el7.x86_64-x86_64-with-centos-7.7.1908-Core Output dir: /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/output Mode: read error correction and assembling Debug mode is turned OFF Dataset parameters: Metagenomic mode Reads: Library number: 1, library type: paired-end orientation: fr left reads: ['/ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/JHW1_BDME200006960-1a_1.fq.gz'] right reads: ['/ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/JHW1_BDME200006960-1a_2.fq.gz'] interlaced reads: not specified single reads: not specified merged reads: not specified Read error correction parameters: Iterations: 1 PHRED offset will be auto-detected Corrected reads will be compressed Assembly parameters: k: [21, 33, 55] Repeat resolution is enabled Mismatch careful mode is turned OFF MismatchCorrector will be SKIPPED Coverage cutoff is turned OFF Other parameters: Dir for temp files: /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/output/tmp Threads: 64 Memory limit (in Gb): 500 ======= SPAdes pipeline started. Log can be found here: /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/output/spades.log ===== Read error correction started. == Running read error correction tool: /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/miniconda3/envs/nanopore/share/spades-3.13.0-0/bin/spades-hammer /ifs1/Grp1/sunruanyang/fc/rawdata/output/corrected/configs/config.info 0:00:00.000 4M / 4M INFO General (main.cpp : 75) Starting BayesHammer, built from refs/heads/spades_3.13.0, git revision 8ea46659e9b2a
output: > > Let is read the log together: > > ``` > > 1:38:31.688 57G / 57G ERROR General (kmer_splitters.hpp : 145) I/O error! Incomplete write! Reason: Disk quota exceeded. Error code: 122 > > ``` > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > > Hopefully the problem is clear > > This problem persists even if there is enough space left. The issue is something else it seems. Please do not trivialize it! Well, this is the error SPAdes received from your operating system. The disk write failed and the reason was provided by the system. There is no way SPAdes could workaround the issue (only terminate itself). You may want to check with your system administrator about quotas, etc.

instruction: Prodigal Exit Code 34304 (CheckM v. 1.2.3) (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hello! instruction: updated taxonomic data for marker sets? (Tool: CheckM)
input: @donovan-h-parks, First off: The Prokaryotic Genome Annotation Pipeline (PGAP) uses CheckM to evaluate the completeness of hundreds of thousands of assemblies. Our use of CheckM has directly improved reference genome selection and enabled the inclusion of MAGs into RefSeq. Thank you! As you may know, there have been many changes to prokaryotic taxa recently: https://ncbiinsights.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/2022/11/14/prokaryotic-phylum-name-changes/ Judging by `checkm_data_2015_01_16.tar.gz`, the names in `taxon_marker_sets.tsv` presumably date from 2015; are there plans to align them with contemporary nomenclature? Regards, George Coulouris National Center for Biotechnology Information (contractor)
output: Indeed. The CheckM v1 and v2 methodologies are very different. In v1, the assessment of genome quality is against a set of presumed single-copy marker genes for a given lineage. CheckM v2 takes a machine learning approach to learn general characteristics about genomes in order to estimate genome quality. As such, this is no concept in CheckM v2 of an assessment being made based on a given taxonomic group. I appreciate this makes CheckM v1 more useful in some cases, and the methods are sufficiently different that they can be seen as two independent estimates of genome quality.

instruction: np.float64 in binstats output causes ast error (fix included) (Tool: CheckM)
input: bin_stats.analyze.tsv contains np.float64: > annotation {'GC': 0.5217391304347826, 'GC std': 0.0, 'Genome size': 87, '# ambiguous bases': 3, '# scaffolds': 1, '# contigs': 1, 'Longest scaffold': 87, 'Longest contig': 84, 'N50 (scaffolds)': 87, 'N50 (contigs)': 84, 'Mean scaffold length': np.float64(87.0), 'Mean contig length': np.float64(84.0), 'Coding density': -1, 'Translation table': -1, '# predicted genes': -1} which ast cannot parse in parseBinStats: ``` Unexpected error: <class 'ValueError'> ... File "/home/coulouri/checkm_venv/lib/python3.9/site-packages/checkm/resultsParser.py", line 157, in parseBinStats binStats[lineSplit[0]] = ast.literal_eval(lineSplit[1]) ... File "/opt/python-3.9/lib/python3.9/ast.py", line 66, in _raise_malformed_node raise ValueError(f'malformed node or string: {node!r}') ``` the following modification to binStatistics.py causes native float types to be emitted rather than numpy float64, which then allows ast to parse successfully: ``` binStats['Mean scaffold length'] = scaffoldAvgLen.self() binStats['Mean contig length'] = contigAvgLen.self() ``` Thanks for fixing!
output: I tried the solution above but I cannot call `self()` from `scaffoldAvgLen` and `contigAvgLen`? ```bash Traceback (most recent call last): File "/home/matinnu/test/bgcflow/.snakemake/conda/9155ccccbb351d3788fd4776665701e0_/lib/python3.11/multiprocessing/process.py", line 314, in _bootstrap self.run() File "/home/matinnu/test/bgcflow/.snakemake/conda/9155ccccbb351d3788fd4776665701e0_/lib/python3.11/multiprocessing/process.py", line 108, in run self._target(*self._args, **self._kwargs) File "/home/matinnu/test/bgcflow/.snakemake/conda/9155ccccbb351d3788fd4776665701e0_/lib/python3.11/site-packages/checkm/binStatistics.py", line 130, in _processBin binStats['Mean scaffold length'] = scaffoldAvgLen.self() ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ AttributeError: 'numpy.float64' object has no attribute 'self' ``` Changing numpy float64 to simple float in `binStatistics.py` also works: ```python binStats['Mean scaffold length'] = float(scaffoldAvgLen) binStats['Mean contig length'] = float(contigAvgLen) ``` <details> <summary>here's the conda environment:</summary> ```yaml name: checkm channels: - instruction: No space left on device error in HMM step not caught (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi there, I ran into an odd bug using CheckM today, where a no space left on device error was not caught and CheckM reported wrong output with exit code 0. I think this is a bit of an edge cases as we have run CheckM on thousands of genomes the last years and this is the first time I'm seeing this, but it did catch us off guard today. We run CheckM within a single rule of our Snakemake-managed workflow. Exit codes >0 are caught and propagated, so that the whole pipeline run is aborted (which we check to monitor technical failures). Today, a colleague using the results of the pipeline notified me that a couple of samples showed very low completeness scores (around 7 or 8), although all other QC metrics were excellent. It seems that `p7_hmmfile.c` ran into a "no space left on device" error for the `/tmp` directory, but that this was not caught by the code calling the command. Locally, I filled up my storage space to try and replicate this error (several tests with 50-200 Mb storage left). The actual HMM error itself was not caught, but in some cases CheckM produced an output file with no exit code >0. With too little storage space, other errors happend before this particular one and with enough space this didn't happen. I'm not familiar with the CheckM source code, so I can't really tell where this should be caught. Happy to open a PR if you have some guidance or an idea where this happens. Please see here for the outptu of runs with limited storage space: [139mb_space.txt](https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/files/14790200/139mb_space.txt) [89mb_space.txt](https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/files/14790201/89mb_space.txt)
output: Hi. I certainly happily accept a PR that ensures a non-zero exit code when this issue occurs.

instruction: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xb0 in position 37: invalid start byte (Tool: CheckM)
input: Just finished installing checkM with all of the required packages and the reference data. When I attempt to run either `> checkm test ~/checkm_test_results` or `> checkm lineage_wf <bin folder> <output folder>` I receive the above error. ('utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xb0 in position 37: invalid start byte. Error: Failed to process sequence file: /home/mossingtonta/checkm/test_data/._637000110.fna). I tried both commands with the reference data and my own fna files, but receive this error for both. I tried removing and reinstalling checkM and the associated packages, but am still encountering this issue.
output: For anybody else who encounters this problem: The issue is that FASTA files should not be in utf-8 encoding format, because this allows for special characters that the program is not equipped to handle. Switching the FASTA files to ASCII format solved this error.

instruction: WARNING: Expected all files to contain sequences in amino acid space. (Tool: CheckM)
input: I am at using checkM at the binning control step with the following commands; `checkm analyze --ali --nt -g -x fa lineage_set_marker.txt ../metaBAT/bins/ out_dir` **[2023-09-30 02:44:48] WARNING: Expected all files to contain sequences in amino acid space. [2023-09-30 02:44:48] WARNING: File ../metaBAT/bins/bin.1.fa appears to contain nucleotide sequences.** is this okay?
output: This was caused by the `-g` flag. Not it is okay.

instruction: maximum number of genomes/MAGs to run checkm (Tool: CheckM)
input: what the title says I have been trying to run checkm on a 64 cores, 500GB RAM machine to get the genome stats for a bit more than 12000 MAGs but it gets stuck at the "Placing 12279 bins into the genome tree with pplacer (be patient)." step. Is it unrealistic to try to run it in more than 3-4k genomes/MAGs? Should I always split in chunks of a few thousand MAGs? whats your recommendation? Thanks
output: Hi. Yes, I recommend splitting the job up into smaller batches: https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/wiki/Bugs-and-Feature-Requests#processing-large-numbers-of-genomes

output: I am having the same error with checkM 1.2.2 (in a singularity). The Dockerfile I intended to use (and push here): ``` FROM ubuntu:latest ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive RUN apt-get update \ && apt install -y git python3-pip hmmer prodigal pplacer wget \ && pip3 install numpy matplotlib pysam checkm-genome RUN wget https://data.ace.uq.edu.au/public/CheckM_databases/checkm_data_2015_01_16.tar.gz && tar -zxvf checkm_data_2015_01_16.tar.gz RUN rm checkm_data_2015_01_16.tar.gz ENV CHECKM_DATA_PATH=/ ENV PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" CMD checkm ``` You can get if with `docker pull 007ptar007/checkm:lastest` Command I ran: ```singularity run --no-home --bind '/home/user/' checkm.sif checkm test ~/testcheckm/``` While trying to look for the file ( if it was misplaced), I noticed that the output is appened to the log file instead of re-writing it, which can be confusing. Also, only the stdout is written and stderr is missing. The first error that happens is actually: ``` [2023-06-21 15:54:41] INFO: CheckM v1.2.2 [2023-06-21 15:54:41] INFO: checkm test /home/user/testcheckm/ [2023-06-21 15:54:41] INFO: CheckM data: / [2023-06-21 15:54:41] INFO: [CheckM - Test] Processing E.coli K12-W3310 to verify operation of CheckM. instruction: Reference data is missing (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hello Where can I find the universal reference genes data set to download?
output: HMMs for the different marker sets used by CheckM can be found in the `hmms` directory of the CheckM reference data. Unfortunately, I don't think there is an easy way to get a particular set of marker genes. All HMMs are stored in a single file (phylo.hmm or checkm.hmm). You would need to extract the HMMs for the marker genes you are interested in.

instruction: AttributeError: 'Namespace' when running 'checkm coverage' (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I ran into the following error message when running 'checkm coverage': > Unexpected error: <class 'AttributeError'> > Traceback (most recent call last): > File "/usr/local/bin/checkm", line 611, in <module> > checkmParser.parseOptions(args) > File "/home/rlhoover/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/checkm/main.py", line 1013, in parseOptions > self.coverage(options) > File "/home/rlhoover/.local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/checkm/main.py", line 739, in coverage > checkBinInputExists(options.bin_input, False) > AttributeError: 'Namespace' object has no attribute 'bin_input' My input was: `checkm coverage ./fasta_files/no006_C20_25_bins/ ./checkm/checkm_coverage ./BAM_files/no006_C20-25.sort.bam -x fa -t 16 ` Which worked when I was running v1.1.0, but hasn't since upgrading to v1.2.2. My files are .fa so I used the -x fa flag. When that didn't work I tried running it on .fna files without the -x flag, but got the same error message. I double checked my file paths and when I run 'checkm test ~/checkm_test_results' everything passes. I'm not sure what else to try and figured it was time to ask the pros. :) Thanks for your help! Cheers, Rene
output: Got it sorted! Something went wrong with my path, but setting it with: `PATH=~/.local/bin:$PATH` before running CheckM resolved the issue.

instruction: Placing 3 bins into the genome tree with pplacer Killed (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi there! I am trying to run some github actions [where CheckM is failling](https://github.com/iquasere/MOSCA/actions/runs/3808833487/jobs/6479815951#step:6:2350), but this doesn't happen when I run the same actions locally. Is this ```Placing 3 bins into the genome tree with pplacer``` step very memory intensive? What could be killing the process, and what could I do to circunmvent it? It's only three bins at that stage that are being profiled, and in the same actions there is an assembly and an annotation of the same data that run fine.
output: Yes, the pplacer step is memory intensive and the peak memory usage is largely independent of the number of bins being placed. You can find system requirements at https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/wiki/Installation#system-requirements.

instruction: The effect of non-prokaryotes sequence (Tool: CheckM)
input: # The effect of non-prokaryotes sequence Dear checkM team, thanks a lot for developing such a wonderful software. Here I have a question, checkm identifies contamination and integrity based on marker genes of prokaryotes, if a high quality bacterial genome is accidentally mixed with sequences of eukaryotes (e.g. fungi, algae or eukaryotic hosts), viruses, then it still assesses high quality, I tested it and it does. Hopefully, the effect of these non-prokaryotes will be considered in the next version. Best wishes!
output: I am a new user of CheckM, but I vaguely remember something about contamination in the output as well. Theoretically, given that backbone of CheckM is a set of HMM marker sets for various taxonomies, one can detect contamination by hitting something belonging to a significantly different marker set. One just needs to pick a suitable workflow I guess

instruction: QA failed not placed in tree - root (Tool: CheckM)
input: Good evening, I have a single circular contig from a cyanobacteria. When I run the standard: `checkm lineage_wf -t 36 -x fasta -f genome_checkm.tab --tab_table /data/folder /data/folder/checkm` I get no markers found, no calculations, no placement in the tree. It is assembled from nanopore but the headers look fine etc. I can pull full length 16S, 23S, and RecA etc. I just trying to get the completeness/contamination stats. Ran a close relative from NCBI in the same run and it worked fine. Lost on why this would be? many thanks, Rick
output: Not sure. Does the gene calling look correct (i.e. expected number of genes of the expected length)? My best guess is that it is an assembly error that is resulting in poor gene calling and thus CheckM (really HMMER) is unable to identify the marker genes.

instruction: Different results between lineage_wf runs (Tool: CheckM)
output: Hi. Sorry for the slow reply. This is a bit surprising, but can happen as CheckM is not deterministic. CheckM places your genome into a reference tree using `pplacer`. This placement can change slightly each time CheckM is run. In general `pplacer` is very stable, but it can happen.

instruction: pplacer never ends (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I started to run checkm, either directly or through drep, on a cluster computer via the command sbatch but every time the process stops when placing bins in the genome tree and eventually the task gets cancelled due to time limit: > [2022-06-20 03:01:56] INFO: Reading marker alignment files. > [2022-06-20 03:01:56] INFO: Concatenating alignments. > [2022-06-20 03:01:56] INFO: Placing 201 bins into the genome tree with pplacer (be patient). > slurmstepd: error: *** JOB 2116389 ON n412-060 CANCELLED AT 2022-06-20T04:59:37 DUE TO TIME LIMIT *** If I check with `top` there is no process running. However, if I run the same commands in local or login node of the cluster it successfully finishes in a few minutes! Any idea why? Does the pplacer step needs ftp access (as I know that this is not possible in our computational nodes)? This is an example of the checkm command I am running: `checkm lineage_wf all_bins/ checkm/ -f checkm/results.tsv -t 88 --pplacer_threads 88 -x fa --tab_table` Thanks in advance for any input!
output: Hi, `pplacer` has a number of interesting "features". We've noticed that it can be problematic to run it with more than 64 CPUs. No real idea why, just something that we have observed. I'd suggest trying it with 64 CPUs and see if this solves the issue. Cheers, Donovan

instruction: Extrapolate USCG sequences from samples (Tool: CheckM)
input: I am attempting to pull identified marker gene nucleotides from my sequences. Context: I am designing a high throughput method to quantify prokaryote biomass, and had previously been using qPCR on the 16S rRNA gene as a proxy for cell numbers. Of course, 16S is variable in gene copy number. When I learned about universal single copy genes (USCG), I started searching for a way to identify highly conserved regions in my samples that are universally present in only one copy. I'd then like to use this data to design primers for qPCR analysis. I have access to several metagenomes, some very deep, from my study site. Question: Can I obtain the USCG sequences from my samples? CheckM seems like it has the information I am looking for, but I haven't been able to pull it out. It seemed like qa output format 9 (FASTA file of marker genes) was close, but I think amino acids are essentially useless for me here. Is there a way to produce a nucleic acid FASTA? My next attempt was to add the --nt flag in the analyze command, but I'm not exactly sure how to interpret this or compare across bins. Maybe the answer for issue #338 would help answer this. Ideally, I would want the nucleotide sequence for USCG X for bins 1-n, USCG Y for bins 1-n, etc. Of course I don't expect this to be laid out so cleanly, but do you think extrapolating this information is possible, and how would you go about it?
output: Hi, I don't believe there is a way to get the nucleotide sequences for the genes used by CheckM. Perhaps something like this would meet your requirements: https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article/37/15/2081/6124302 Cheers, Donovan

instruction: guppy: loadlocale.c:129 (Tool: CheckM)
input: We had this strange error encountered in checkM, I have a possible solution and wanted to mention it to you. https://github.com/metagenome-atlas/atlas/issues/525
output: Hi Silas, Guppy is part of the pplacer package. I haven't run across either of the issues you've pointed out. The most common issue we see with pplacer is systems running out of RAM. Cheers, Donovan

instruction: checkm python compatibility (<v.3.7.12) (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hello, It's worth noting after the recent pull request and the cpython issue that occurs in versions of Python3 >= 3.8 that with the current release, you can only run checkm on python3 versions of 3.7 (with fasta files that have Unix formatted line endings). I'm just posting this to save other people headaches with installation and usage. Thank you for this great tool! Will
output: Hi Will, Sorry - can you confirm this limitation of the Python version is strictly due to the PR by misialq. I don't plan to accept this PR if it is restricting the version of Python 3 that must be used. I appreciate this probably isn't the best for OS X users, but I'm hesitant to force everyone to use a specific version of Python. Thanks.

input: Hello, I installed CheckM through conda in a non-default directory and followed exactly the same steps given on the wiki page. instruction: inverse_transformed deprecated (Tool: CheckM)
input: I was attempting to run marker_plot and ran into an issue `AttributeError: 'BBox' has no attribute 'inverse_transformed'`. I believe this issue is because inverse_transformed has been deprecated (see [here](https://matplotlib.org/3.3.3/api/api_changes.html#bboxbase-inverse-transformed)). Only the required positional arguments were provided to inverse_transformed.
output: You will either have to downgrade to Matplotlib<3.3 (I believe that's when it was deprecated) or just made tiny adjustments in `AbstractPlot.py`. Change `bboxi = bbox.inverse_transformed(self.fig.transFigure)` to `bboxi = bbox.transformed(self.fig.transFigure.inverted())` on lines 81, 88, 106, and 124 (CheckM v1.1.3).

instruction: checkm setRoot not working (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hello, I am using checkm as part of a data pipeline in nextflow (called MUFFIN) and checkm data setRoot does not seem to set the location properly. Thus far, I've tried `checkm data setRoot /path/to/checkm/db` as a super user and normally (which was in a previous issue), I've interrupted `checkm data setRoot /path/to/checkm/db` and re-run it (which I read in an older bug report), and I have copied the .dmanifest file to every single home folder in the pipeline and used the `-resume` feature (which wouldn't work anyways because nextflow creates new folders for `-resume`). In every case, CheckM assumes it has never been used and proceeds to run the `checkm data setRoot` process anyways, which causes an EOF error in nextflow. The result is as follows: ``` [skipping] Stored process > sourmash_download_db [skipping] Stored process > checkm_download_db Error executing process > 'refine2 (1)' Caused by: Process `refine2 (1)` terminated with an error exit status (1) Command executed: mem=$(echo 490 GB | sed 's/g//g') path_db=$(cat path_db.txt) echo $path_db checkm data setRoot $path_db echo "checkm done" metawrap bin_refinement -o refined_bins -A bins_dir -B fasta_bins -o refined_bins -t 120 -m $mem mkdir metawrap_bins/ mv refined_bins/metawrap_70_10_bins/*.fa metawrap_bins/ mv refined_bins/metawrap_70_10_bins.stats subset_binning_stats.txt Command exit status: 1 Command output: /home/ubuntu/nextflow-autodownload-databases/checkm/db It seems that the CheckM data folder has not been set yet or has been removed. Running: 'checkm data setRoot'. Where should CheckM store it's data? Please specify a location or type 'abort' to stop trying: Command error: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/home/ubuntu/work/conda/env-365fdf3d23c18ec576559d837f2bcac1/bin/checkm", line 36, in <module> from checkm import main File "/home/ubuntu/work/conda/env-365fdf3d23c18ec576559d837f2bcac1/lib/python2.7/site-packages/checkm/main.py", line 25, in <module> from checkm.defaultValues import Defa
output: I figured out a solution. My solution was to edit the DATA_CONFIG file located at `/.../site-packages/checkm/DATA_CONFIG` I replaced the entry corresponding to `"dataRoot"` to `"path/to/checkm/db"` and it worked just fine afterwards -- so I'd assume that's the manual equivalent of using `checkm data setRoot path/to/checkm/db`

instruction: Contamination is not in precentage? (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, After following the linear_wf, I got the qa result. But I found that in the column of Contamination, somoe number can be larger than 100. What does this number mean? And how can we convert it to the percentage? Thanks! Zewei ![图片](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7750107/146679969-8158901a-f988-417c-b885-81c80e90722e.png)
output: Contamination is a percentage it is just not bounded to 100%. Full details on the contamination calculation used by CheckM can be found in the CheckM manuscript.

instruction: Sharing databases on a cluster and .dmanifest problem... (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hello! I'm teaching on a cluster and I'm trying to get other users to install checkM (which works I think), but then I also wanted to share the precalculated database (it doesn't seem logic to have everyone download it on their own). I'm having a lot of problems, since they can read and access it but then when running `> checkm data setRoot <checkm_data_dir>` it generates a `.dmanifest` file per user, hence it tries to overwrite it each time a new user sets the path... could I have some input on what this file is doing? can I let everyone overwrite it? or maybe get my install version so everyone uses the same? does it ineed to be personalized? thanks !
output: Hi. The individual who setup the database end of CheckM has moved on to other ventures. I'm not that familiar with the exact role of the .dmanifest file. I think it should work so long as the path is staying the same. I'd certainly try testing it out first though. Alternatively, CheckM can be install system wide for all users. I think this is how most labs are using CheckM.

instruction: Problem calculating quality (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi! I'm checking the quality of a set of publicly available genomes, but when assessing the contamination and completeness of them, none of the genomes have >50% of completeness **&** <5% contamination. I checked the .log files but no problem was reported. Could you help me with this? I wonder if it's a problem with the format of the .fna files: I checked with CheckM the quality of another set of genomes and the results were ok. I attach a picture of one of the genomes that are problematic as an example. I'm using v 1.1.3 (latest version if I'm not wrong). ![EXAMPLE](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/91469300/134901216-a9f0b009-999d-4733-ad95-3adc791d0082.png) Thanks for the help!
output: When I check with $file /filename/, all my .fna files are ASCII text, so I suppose that this implies that they are Unix coded. Right? I wonder also if the sequences of the contigs need to be on the same line instead of broken into different lines within the .fna files. Could this be relevant?

Unexpected error: <type 'exceptions.AttributeError'> Traceback (most recent call last): File "/data2/lcy/miniconda3/envs/metawrap-env/bin/checkm", line 708, in <module> checkmParser.parseOptions(args) File "/data2/lcy/miniconda3/envs/metawrap-env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/checkm/main.py", line 1253, in parseOptions self.analyze(options) File "/data2/lcy/miniconda3/envs/metawrap-env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/checkm/main.py", line 331, in analyze os.path.join(options.out_folder, 'storage', 'aai_qa') File "/data2/lcy/miniconda3/envs/metawrap-env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/checkm/hmmerAligner.py", line 176, in makeAlignmentsOfMultipleHits p.terminate() File "/data2/lcy/miniconda3/envs/metawrap-env/lib/python2.7/multiprocessing/process.py", line 140, in terminate self._popen.terminate() AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'terminate' is it too much genomes input that cause the error ?
output: Hi. The number of bins is acceptable assuming your machine has sufficient memory. I usually run genomes in batches of 1,000 just to ensure any issues (e.g., ill-formatted input data) do not result in the entire run failing. The problem does appear to be with CheckM, but perhaps this is a result of how the data is handle by metawrap prior to being executed. I'm not familiar enough with metawrap to know if this could be an issue.

instruction: [Question] Does `checkm lineage_wf` use CPR_43 marker set for Patescibacteria? (Tool: CheckM)
input: I had a question regarding your recent update: https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/wiki/Workflows#using-cpr-marker-set If one is running `checkm lineage_wf` and a Patescibacteria is found, is the marker set automatically [CPR_43](https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/blob/master/custom_marker_sets/cpr_43_markers.hmm) for this? Also, I think https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/tree/master/custom_marker_sets/cpr_43_markers.xlxs is mispelled and is supposed to be cpr_43_markers.xlsx. I downloaded it and confirmed it was an excel doc.
output: Good call. I took a look at it and I don't know the mechanics of CheckM well enough to do it justice. I made a work around for one of my scripts. I ran lineage_wf on a bunch of bona fide CPR and almost ever single one came back as basal bacteria lineage for the marker set. I just set an argument to include these when running my completion/contamination filters so I can adjust post hoc. Looking forward to CheckM version 2. Such an iconic piece of software in metagenomics. If you ever need a 3rd party tester, I have a lot of datasets at JCVI I can run it on.

instruction: Unclear functioning of the `checkm merge` function (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I'm using checkm for my pipeline, and I understood most of the steps that you perform on your reference paper. However, one thing confuses me: after running `checkm merge`, to get the information of which bins could be merged, and provided that I am ok with merging them: how do I *actually* merge them? Should I write my own script to do it, or is there a tool inside checkm that does it for me? So far, I haven't seen it, so please if you could be so kind to indicate me the best way to proceed! Thanks :)
output: Thanks for the answer! That's what I thought as well, but I think from a user perspective it would be useful if you could make it more obvious. Thanks!

instruction: input file type (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi. I want to use CheckM to check and remove high-quality bacterial sequences in my genomic fasta file. (because my fasta file should not be including bacterial sequences.) I wonder is CheckM acceptable only bin file, not simple fasta file for input? especially ```lineage_wf``` option. Best Yun
output: CheckM expects the contigs/scaffolds in a FASTA file to be from a MAG, SAG, or isolate. It then estimates the quality of this genome. The output of CheckM doesn't make sense if you provide it with a FASTA file containing all contigs from a metagenomic assembly.

instruction: Using CheckM for one fasta file directly (Tool: CheckM)
input: Is it possible/combination of options to run checksum for one sequence without mentioning a folder where the genomes are stored? F. ex. checkm lineage_wf -x fa GCF_000196035.1_ASM19603v1_genomic.fa Output/
output: Ok, Thanks. Any quick tips if I want to modify the code to support this? I want to add this tool to our local galaxy. People want to use it for single genomes.

instruction: marker sets in fasta format? (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I probably missed something, are the marker gene sets come with the checkM available in fasta format somewhere? I am aware that qa with -o 9 will output the protein sequences from a specified analysis, but I hope to check up on the raw marker sets. Thank you!
output: Hi, You can use `checkm qa -o4 ./checkm_lineage_wf/lineage.ms ./checkm_lineage_wf -f qa4.tsv` to get the marker set used by each genome. CheckM uses TIGRFAM and Pfam HMMs. You can visit these resources to get the HMMs and additional information about these protein families. Cheers, Donovan

instruction: lineage_wf: OSError from multiprocessing module when --tmpdir specified (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I used checkM v1.1.3 installed in a conda base environment on ubuntu/centOS7 servers with `checkm lineage_wf -t 32 --pplacer_threads 32 -x fa /work/input/ checkmResult`. This will finish with no problem. However, the default tmp directory on the server has a very limited space, so for some runs I tried specifying `--tmpdir /work/tmp` to a regular folder owned by me, which gave the following STD log with errors: ``` [2021-05-13 03:35:19] INFO: CheckM v1.1.3 [2021-05-13 03:35:19] INFO: checkm lineage_wf -t 32 --tmpdir /work/tmp/ --pplacer_threads 32 -x fa /work/prj/dev_hic paper/myrun/bin3c_clust/part1d/ checkM_bin3c_part1d [2021-05-13 03:35:19] INFO: [CheckM - tree] Placing bins in reference genome tree. [2021-05-13 03:35:24] INFO: Identifying marker genes in 155 bins with 32 threads: Finished processing 155 of 155 (100.00%) bins. Traceback (most recent call last): File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/process.py", line 297, in _bootstrap self.run() File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/process.py", line 99, in run self._target(*self._args, **self._kwargs) File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/managers.py", line 597, in _run_server server.serve_forever() File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/managers.py", line 173, in serve_forever sys.exit(0) SystemExit: 0 During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/util.py", line 277, in _run_finalizers finalizer() File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/util.py", line 201, in __call__ res = self._callback(*self._args, **self._kwargs) File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/multiprocessing/util.py", line 110, in _remove_temp_dir rmtree(tempdir) File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/shutil.py", line 494, in rmtree _rmtree_safe_fd(fd, path, onerror) File "/work/anaconda3/lib/python3.7/shutil.py", line 452, in _rmtree_safe_fd onerror(os.unlink, fullname, sys.ex
output: Not a solution but in case anyone lands here in the future: This is because the tmp directory used was NFS mounted, not because specifying --tmpdir or input size. I'm leaving the title & post as-is but please feel free to curate. In my case, CheckM will proceed regardless. I think the results are not affected as the error appears to only happen when python's multiprocessing manager finishes jobs and does the clean up & shutdown sequence; might not hold if CheckM tries to clean its tmp files midway, however.

instruction: About coding base of Prodigal.py (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, Can you check the last line of Prodigal.py? **_return np.sum(self.codingBaseMasks[seqId][start:end])_** I think it produces a little error since ignoring the lastCodingBase of each contig (or scaffold). If it is true, it will be better to replace "end" as "end+1" or fix the line 227. Thank you
output: **Thank you for answer!** I understand about this occurs by 0 vs 1 indexing issue. However, I still hard to understand **why the different rule is applied for only the last gene of contig**? I enclosed a simple example below, if 1-based indexing gff file given like below, _contig gene start end contig1 gene1 1 3 contig1 gene2 5 7_ here I exclude accessary information of gff files like as annotation tools or type of gene, strand, etc I expected 6 coding bases from this gff file (or single contig) but in the prodigal.py genes = [(1,3), (5,7)] // in line 226 // it is simply modifed since dictionary used in original code lastCodingBase = 7 // in line 227 then, codingBaseMask = [0,1,1,1,0,1,1] // by line 234-236 // Expected [0,1,1,1,0,1,1,1] but np.zeros(7) make shorter result then, np.sum(codingBaseMask[0:7]) of line 251 resulting 5 instead of 6 I expected. I'm just asking because I detected a little different result between own coded script and checkM, so I hope you don't feel unpleasant by this bug reporting.

instruction: CheckM marker sets (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I have two questions regarding the `taxon_marker_sets.tsv` file: 1. There are two species which contain empty sets in the `marker_sets` field: _Prevotella melaninogenic_ and _Clostridium acetobutylicum_. Is there a reason for this? 2. In the `checkm.hmm` profiles theres quite a few profiles (I counted 1493) that are nowhere in the `taxon_marker_sets.tsv` and/or `tigrfam2pfam.tsv`, even when using the `tigrfam2pfam.tsv` file to convert between IDs. Why is that? I do find 13 of them in `genome_tree.metadata.tsv` though and these are also found in the `tigrfam2pfam.tsv`. Thanks for all the great work! Best Oskar
output: Hi Oskar, The `taxon_marker_set.tsv` and `checkm.hmm` files aren't designed to be manually inspected. What information are you trying to obtain? The `taxon_marker_set.tsv` file does contain information for both _Prevotella melaninogenica_ (not additional `a`) and _Clostridium acetobutylicum_ (see below). Is the `checkm.hmm` file the results of running the `lineage_wf`? This will have marker sets for internal nodes that are not related to specific taxa. Cheers, Donovan ``` species Prevotella melaninogenica Bacteria;Bacteroidetes;Bacteroidia;Bacteroidales;Prevotellaceae;Prevotella;melaninogenica 2 1041 226 [set(['PF05188.12', 'PF00550.20',.... species Clostridium acetobutylicum Bacteria;Firmicutes;Clostridia;Clostridiales;Peptostreptococcaceae;Clostridium;acetobutylicum 3 1498 243 [set(['PF04297.9', 'PF01192.17', .... ```

instruction: Too many files and recursion problem (Tool: CheckM)
input: This is valid for `v1.1.3` This problem [was already reported in 2017 (it's closed)](https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/issues/118) but It's still present. I just posted it there and I noticed that a user already reported it again in 2019. It would be very useful to have this information in the wiki/help, because a lot of time/computation is wasted because of it. In my case the problem was triggered analyzing 2300 genomes.
output: I am sorry to hear you have encounter this issue. I have added the recursion issue to the CheckM wiki page at: https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/wiki/Bugs-and-Feature-Requests

instruction: checkM hangs with reduced_tree option (Tool: CheckM)
input: In some situations checkM lineage_wf hangs indefinitely at the analyze step ("Identifying marker genes in bins."), even when using relatively high-quality genomes from NCBI RefSeq. The reported issue only happens when using the option --reduced_tree, but regardless of whether an AA fasta is provided (with option --genes) or a DNA fasta is provided. **Details** The problem manifests inside function setLineageSpecificSelectedMarkerSet, defined at line 95 of file markerSets.py. It seems that checkM gets caught in some sort of infinite while loop (the one define on line 102). Using checkM 1.1.3 with python 3.7 on MacOS 10.13.6, 128 GB RAM. The issue can be reproduced on my end using the attached fasta (placed unzipped inside the directory "input_DNA") and the command: `checkm lineage_wf --quiet --reduced_tree --tab_table -x fasta --file summary.tsv input_DNA output_DNA` [GCF_000752275.1.fasta.gz](https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/files/6145811/GCF_000752275.1.fasta.gz)
output: Yes, I just tried without reduced tree flag, it works for analyze step. It is a bit strange that some genomes from NCBI RefSeq worked fine with reduced tree flag, but some are not (hangs at the analyze step). May I ask whether the results are comparable with or without reduced tree flag, as I have checked part of genomes with reduced tree flag?

instruction: Get details of marker genes for genomes (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I am probably missing some obvious info but I can't find a way to get details about marker genes found in a genome analyzed with the `lineage_wf` workflow, _ie_: - How do I get to know which markers were found one time, which ones were found two times etc? - How do I get the genome coordinates of these markers in a text file? output: Hi. The `qa` command can be used to produce different output tables: https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/wiki/Genome-Quality-Commands#qa I think you are after `table 4` and maybe `table 5`.

instruction: Does the lineage_wf include checkm tree_qa? (Tool: CheckM)
input: In the tutorial you mention the steps: (M) > checkm tree bin folder output folder (R) > checkm tree_qa output folder (M) > checkm lineage_set output folder marker file (M) > checkm analyze marker file bin folder output folder (M) > checkm qa marker file output folder For convenience, the above workflow can be executed in a single step: > checkm lineage_wf bin folder output folder I just checked my log file and I don't see the checkm tree_qa step. Is that not included in the lineage_wf?
output: Hi. You are correct. The lineage_wf does not run tree_qa. I've modified the Wiki to clarify this.

instruction: Estimating contamination and genome completeness version 1.1.2 vs 1.0.5 (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I am currently using checkM v1.1.2 to analyze the quality of recovered genomes belonging to a specific phylum from 2 metagenomic samples. A previous study had already recovered similar genomes from the same 2 metagenomic samples and were assessed using checkM v1.0.5 back in 2017. I would like to know if checkM's algorithm for estimating % completion, % contamination and strain heterogeneity from running checkm qa in version 1.1.2 is similar to that of version 1.0.5? If there aren't major changes with how those metrics are calculated between the two versions I am hoping to be able to compare my recovered genomes to the 2017 genomes using the qa metrics I've listed above. Thank you in advance for your help. :)
output: Hi. There are no substantive changes between v1.0.5 and 1.1.2. The results *may* not be 100% identical since CheckM (namely the pplacer step) is not deterministic, but this is independent of the CheckM version used.

instruction: Checkm data setRoot problem (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, Apologies if this is a commonly reported issue, or if I'm just doing something simple wrong (I'm a bit of a bioinformatics newby). I had installed CheckM onto the base environment but decided to uninstall it and reinstall it into a dedicated conda environment. However, when I try to specify the directory for the checkm data using the following command: _sudo checkm data setRoot /xxx/xxx/checkm_data_ I get the following output: _It seems that the CheckM data folder has not been set yet or has been removed. Please run 'checkm data setRoot'. Path [/home/ubuntu/.checkm] exists but you do not have permission to write to this folder._ I'm not really sure what's happening here and any help would be greatly appreciated. I think the problem may be related to the fact that I installed checkm previously into the 'base' environment'. Kind regards, Bruce
output: Hi, Starting with CheckM v1.1.6 the CHECKM_DATA_PATH environmental variable can be used to indicate the path to the CheckM reference data: https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/wiki/Installation#required-reference-data Cheers, Donovan

instruction: Help reading the output of checkm... What are the .pkl.gz files on the storage? (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi, I am exploring checkm, but I need some help interpreting the output. I run: checkm lineage_wf -t 8 -x fasta . ./checkm I have the following questions: - Of all files produced, which one will be a summary for the lineage of my contigs? Is there any table that indicate the closest lineage for each scaffold or contig? - will the above usage produce any output plots? - what are the .pkl.gz files on the storage folder? Thanks, Ricardo
output: Hi. CheckM does not produce a file indicating the lineage of each contig. Each genome is placed in a reference tree and a taxonomic classification assigned to the genome. I do not recommend using these taxonomic assignments in general though as they are only meant to establish the best marker set to use for assessing completeness and contamination. There are separate commands for producing various plots (see: https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM/wiki). The *.pkl.gz files contain information needed between different CheckM commands and aren't meant to be used by researchers.

instruction: File not found error in checkm (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hi! I've tried running it in multiple directories and multiple conda environments but it keeps raising the same error. I've also tried it both on the test data and on my own data. There was an issue in prior CheckM versions related to how the latest release of Prodigal handles some FASTA files that has impacted a number of users so perhaps this also explains your results.

instruction: Bug with checkm profile -- and possible fix (Tool: CheckM)
input: Hello, Running checkm v. 1.1.2, installed via Conda. Doing some relative abundance calculations using a combination of `checkm coverage` + `checkm profile`. However, when running `checkm profile`, I get this error: ``` [2020-08-10 10:27:23] INFO: CheckM v1.1.2 [2020-08-10 10:27:23] INFO: checkm profile -f profile.txt checkm_coverage_output.tsv [2020-08-10 10:27:23] INFO: [CheckM - profile] Calculating percentage of reads mapped to each bin. [2020-08-10 10:27:23] INFO: Determining number of reads mapped to each bin. Traceback (most recent call last): File "/home/tbiondi/.conda/envs/checkm/bin/checkm", line 611, in <module> checkmParser.parseOptions(args) File "/home/tbiondi/.conda/envs/checkm/lib/python3.7/site-packages/checkm/main.py", line 895, in parseOptions self.profile(options) File "/home/tbiondi/.conda/envs/checkm/lib/python3.7/site-packages/checkm/main.py", line 674, in profile profile.run(options.coverage_file, options.file, options.bTabTable) File "/home/tbiondi/.conda/envs/checkm/lib/python3.7/site-packages/checkm/profile.py", line 109, in run pTable = prettytable.PrettyTable(header) NameError: name 'prettytable' is not defined ``` THE FIX: The issue points towards line 109 in profile.py (as seen above.) The fix is as follows: In profile.py, change ``` import checkm.prettytable ``` to ``` import checkm.prettytable as prettytable ``` Then it works fine. This is the same problem mentioned in #258, which was closed.
output: Sorry for the slow fix. This issue has been resolved in CheckM v1.1.7 which will be released shortly.
